 ## Structure
@@ -9,18 +9,20 @@ Keep changes narrow, evidence-backed, and automation-safe.

+Preserve one flat `items/` and `closed/` report layout per repository slug. Do
+not split reports into issue/PR subtrees.
 
 ## Operating Model
 
 - Review lane is proposal-only. It never closes GitHub items.
 - Apply lane mutates GitHub by syncing the durable Codex review comment and then
   closing only unchanged, high-confidence proposals.
+- Repository-specific rules live in `src/repository-profiles.ts`; ClawHub apply
+  may close only PRs that are certainly implemented on `main`.
 - Worker concurrency is shard-level: each shard processes its selected items
   sequentially. Maximum parallel Codex sessions equals `shard_count`, not
   `batch_size * shard_count`.

+## Decision
+
+Close: belongs on ClawHub
+
+Confidence: high

+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Useful idea, but current main already exposes the right lower-level primitives for this as a ClawHub skill/plugin rather than a new OpenClaw core feature.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Vision steers optional orchestration out of core:** Project direction says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, new skills should go to ClawHub first, and OpenClaw will not merge agent-hierarchy frameworks or heavy orchestration layers that duplicate existing agent/tool infrastructure.
+  - file: [VISION.md:54](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/VISION.md#L54)
+  - command: `nl -ba VISION.md | sed -n '35,125p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Core already has session coordination primitives:** Current docs expose `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, `sessions_send`, `sessions_spawn`, `sessions_yield`, `subagents`, and `session_status`, including cross-session messaging, waiting for replies, and sub-agent orchestration.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/session-tool.md:15](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/concepts/session-tool.md#L15)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/concepts/session-tool.md | sed -n '1,170p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Subagent docs already position this as parallel background work:** Sub-agents are documented as the parallel/background work mechanism, with nested orchestrator patterns, thread-bound sessions, steering, and completion delivery back to the requester.
+  - file: [docs/tools/subagents.md:10](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/tools/subagents.md#L10)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/tools/subagents.md | sed -n '1,230p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Plugin SDK already supports building higher-level orchestration:** Plugin runtime exposes `api.runtime.subagent` for launching/managing subagent runs and `api.runtime.taskFlow` for durable orchestration state over tasks/flows.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md:87](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md#L87)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md | sed -n '80,150p'`

+- **Task Flow provides durable shared flow state:** Task Flow is documented and implemented as the orchestration substrate above background tasks, with managed flow state, revision tracking, inspection, and cancellation.
+  - file: [docs/automation/taskflow.md:10](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/automation/taskflow.md#L10)

+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Core roadmap could still choose to add first-class team UX later if maintainers want a built-in opinionated layer on top of these primitives.
+- Closing this issue does not evaluate the separate open orchestration proposals/issues referenced in the thread; those may still be valid in their own scoped forms.

+## Close Comment
+
+Closing this as better suited for [ClawHub](https://clawhub.ai/)/community plugin work after Codex review.
+
+Useful idea, but current main already exposes the right lower-level primitives for this as a ClawHub skill/plugin rather than a new OpenClaw core feature.
+
+What I checked:
+
+- **Vision steers optional orchestration out of core:** [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md): Project direction says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, new skills should go to ClawHub first, and OpenClaw will not merge agent-hierarchy frameworks or heavy orchestration layers that duplicate existing agent/tool infrastructure. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/VISION.md#L54), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+- **Core already has session coordination primitives:** Current docs expose `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, `sessions_send`, `sessions_spawn`, `sessions_yield`, `subagents`, and `session_status`, including cross-session messaging, waiting for replies, and sub-agent orchestration. Public docs: [`docs/concepts/session-tool.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/session-tool). ([`docs/concepts/session-tool.md:15`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/session-tool), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+- **Subagent docs already position this as parallel background work:** Sub-agents are documented as the parallel/background work mechanism, with nested orchestrator patterns, thread-bound sessions, steering, and completion delivery back to the requester. Public docs: [`docs/tools/subagents.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/subagents). ([`docs/tools/subagents.md:10`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/subagents), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+- **Plugin SDK already supports building higher-level orchestration:** Plugin runtime exposes `api.runtime.subagent` for launching/managing subagent runs and `api.runtime.taskFlow` for durable orchestration state over tasks/flows. Public docs: [`docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/sdk-runtime). ([`docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md:87`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/sdk-runtime), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+- **Task Flow provides durable shared flow state:** Task Flow is documented and implemented as the orchestration substrate above background tasks, with managed flow state, revision tracking, inspection, and cancellation. Public docs: [`docs/automation/taskflow.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/taskflow). ([`docs/automation/taskflow.md:10`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/taskflow), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+- **Current `sessions_send` already supports peer delivery semantics:** Implementation supports sending to another visible session, waiting for reply, and an agent-to-agent reply-back loop, which covers the low-level peer messaging piece without a new dedicated core teams surface. ([`src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts:233`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts#L233), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+
+So I’m closing this as a scope-fit item for the plugin/community path rather than keeping it open as an OpenClaw core request.

+# [#10067: Feature: Cron job catch-up for missed runs after gateway restart](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10067)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10067](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10067)
+
+Author: TeigenZhang
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 6, 2026, 03:02 UTC
+
+Updated at: Feb 10, 2026, 07:26 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.3.7](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.3.7)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main already implements restart catch-up for recurring cron jobs: startup clears stale running markers, runs missed recurring jobs immediately, replays missed cron slots based on lastRunAtMs, and staggers deferred backlog jobs. The request's underlying behavior is shipped, although it landed as built-in restart recovery rather than an opt-in `catchUp` schedule flag.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Startup path runs catch-up on service start:** Cron startup clears stale running markers, skips replay only for interrupted one-shot jobs, then calls `runMissedJobs(...)` before arming the normal timer.
+  - file: [src/cron/service/ops.ts:93](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/cron/service/ops.ts#L93)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cron/service/ops.ts | sed -n '87,132p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Catch-up logic replays missed recurring jobs and cron slots:** `runMissedJobs` collects runnable overdue jobs at startup with `allowCronMissedRunByLastRun: true`, logs `cron: running missed jobs after restart`, executes immediate candidates, and staggers deferred jobs.
+  - file: [src/cron/service/timer.ts:962](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/cron/service/timer.ts#L962)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cron/service/timer.ts | sed -n '885,1120p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Regression tests cover the reported scenario:** Tests assert that an overdue recurring cron job executes immediately on start, interrupted recurring jobs replay on restart, missed cron slots replay after restart, and interrupted one-shot jobs do not replay.
+  - file: [src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts:79](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts#L79)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts | sed -n '1,220p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Feature shipped in release notes:** Changelog for `2026.3.7` says: `Cron/restart catch-up semantics: replay interrupted recurring jobs and missed immediate cron slots on startup without replaying interrupted one-shot jobs...`
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:2982](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/CHANGELOG.md#L2982)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '2968,2990p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Follow-up shipped for backlog staggering:** Changelog for `2026.3.8` says: `Cron/restart catch-up staggering: limit immediate missed-job replay on startup and reschedule the deferred remainder...`
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:2852](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/CHANGELOG.md#L2852)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '2790,2875p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **No opt-in schedule flag exists on main:** Current cron schedule type supports `expr`, `tz`, and `staggerMs`; there is no `catchUp` field. The request was implemented as default recurring-job restart recovery rather than a per-job flag.
+  - file: [src/cron/types.ts:6](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/cron/types.ts#L6)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cron/types.ts | sed -n '1,40p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The issue body specifically proposed an opt-in `catchUp` flag, but current main implements catch-up behavior unconditionally for recurring jobs rather than exposing that flag.
+- Docs in `docs/automation/cron-jobs.md` describe persistence and scheduling but do not explicitly call out restart catch-up semantics, so user discoverability may still be imperfect.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
+
+Current main already implements restart catch-up for recurring cron jobs: startup clears stale running markers, runs missed recurring jobs immediately, replays missed cron slots based on lastRunAtMs, and staggers deferred backlog jobs. The request's underlying behavior is shipped, although it landed as built-in restart recovery rather than an opt-in `catchUp` schedule flag.
+
+What I checked:
+
+- **Startup path runs catch-up on service start:** Cron startup clears stale running markers, skips replay only for interrupted one-shot jobs, then calls `runMissedJobs(...)` before arming the normal timer. ([`src/cron/service/ops.ts:93`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/src/cron/service/ops.ts#L93), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+- **Catch-up logic replays missed recurring jobs and cron slots:** `runMissedJobs` collects runnable overdue jobs at startup with `allowCronMissedRunByLastRun: true`, logs `cron: running missed jobs after restart`, executes immediate candidates, and staggers deferred jobs. ([`src/cron/service/timer.ts:962`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/src/cron/service/timer.ts#L962), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+- **Regression tests cover the reported scenario:** Tests assert that an overdue recurring cron job executes immediately on start, interrupted recurring jobs replay on restart, missed cron slots replay after restart, and interrupted one-shot jobs do not replay. ([`src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts:79`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts#L79), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+- **Feature shipped in release notes:** Changelog for `2026.3.7` says: `Cron/restart catch-up semantics: replay interrupted recurring jobs and missed immediate cron slots on startup without replaying interrupted one-shot jobs...`. ([`CHANGELOG.md:2982`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/CHANGELOG.md#L2982), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+- **Follow-up shipped for backlog staggering:** Changelog for `2026.3.8` says: `Cron/restart catch-up staggering: limit immediate missed-job replay on startup and reschedule the deferred remainder...`. ([`CHANGELOG.md:2852`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/CHANGELOG.md#L2852), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+- **No opt-in schedule flag exists on main:** Current cron schedule type supports `expr`, `tz`, and `staggerMs`; there is no `catchUp` field. The request was implemented as default recurring-job restart recovery rather than a per-job flag. ([`src/cron/types.ts:6`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/src/cron/types.ts#L6), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
+
+Review notes: reviewed against [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a); fix evidence: release [v2026.3.7](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.3.7).

The same implementation is present in the latest shipped release `v2026.4.22`.

+## Evidence

+- **Package script uses Node, not bash:** `canvas:a2ui:bundle` is `node scripts/bundle-a2ui.mjs` on current main.

+- **Windows-specific pnpm execution exists:** `resolvePnpmRunner` uses `cmd.exe /d /s /c` with `pnpm.cmd` and `windowsVerbatimArguments: true` on `win32`, which addresses the Windows shell path called out in the issue.

+- **Windows behavior is covered by tests:** There is an explicit test asserting the Windows `cmd.exe` + `pnpm.cmd` runner behavior.
+  - file: [test/scripts/pnpm-runner.test.ts:109](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/test/scripts/pnpm-runner.test.ts#L109)

+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)

+## Evidence

+- **Quoted parent message is fetched from Feishu when `parent_id` is present:** The Feishu bot handler calls `getMessageFeishu({ messageId: ctx.parentId, ... })` and stores the returned content in `quotedContent` when visibility rules allow it.
+  - file: [extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts:728](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts#L728)

+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Quoted content is inserted into the text the agent sees:** `buildFeishuAgentBody` rewrites the agent-facing message as `[Replying to: "${quotedContent}"]\n\n${ctx.content}` before adding sender metadata.
+  - file: [extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts:157](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts#L157)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts | sed -n '146,188p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Inbound context explicitly sends the quote to the agent:** The finalized inbound payload uses `BodyForAgent: messageBody` and also sets `ReplyToId`, `RootMessageId`, and `ReplyToBody`, so the agent path no longer falls back to raw `CommandBody`.
+  - file: [extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts:983](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts#L983)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts | sed -n '983,1003p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Tests cover reply/quote propagation:** Feishu tests assert that reply events propagate `ReplyToId`, `RootMessageId`, and `ReplyToBody: "quoted content"` into inbound context.
+  - file: [extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts:745](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts#L745)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts | sed -n '720,820p'`

+- **Runtime prefers `BodyForAgent` over envelope `Body`:** The auto-reply session-state tests verify that agent processing uses `BodyForAgent` rather than `Body`, which is the key behavior needed for the Feishu fix to reach the model.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts:710](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts#L710)

+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)

+# [#10149: [Feature]: Adopt @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk for enhanced agent capabilities](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10149)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10149](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10149)
+
+Author: eciuca
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 6, 2026, 05:50 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 19, 2026, 05:32 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: belongs on ClawHub
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Issue requests a core Anthropic Claude Agent SDK migration, but current main documents this class of runtime as a plugin/provider-harness concern rather than OpenClaw core. The repo has explicit plugin and agent-harness extension points for native agent daemons, and VISION.md says optional capability should usually ship as plugins while heavy orchestration layers duplicating existing agent/tool infrastructure are out of scope for core.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Vision prefers plugins over core for optional capability:** VISION.md says core stays lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high.
+  - file: [VISION.md:54](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/VISION.md#L54)
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Vision excludes duplicate orchestration layers from core roadmap:** VISION.md lists heavy orchestration layers that duplicate existing agent and tool infrastructure among things OpenClaw will not merge for now.
+  - file: [VISION.md:99](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/VISION.md#L99)
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Provider plugin docs route native agent runtimes out of core:** docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md says that if a model must run through a native agent daemon that owns threads, compaction, or tool events, it should pair a provider with an agent harness instead of putting daemon protocol details in core.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md:22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md#L22)
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Agent harness docs define the intended extension point:** docs/plugins/sdk-agent-harness.md says harnesses are for native session runtimes such as local CLI/daemon integrations and explicitly says not to use core changes for normal provider additions.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/sdk-agent-harness.md:20](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/plugins/sdk-agent-harness.md#L20)
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **ClawHub/community plugin path exists for provider integrations:** docs/tools/plugin.md documents external community plugins and ClawHub installation, and docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md notes provider plugins can be published on ClawHub.
+  - file: [docs/tools/plugin.md:11](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/tools/plugin.md#L11)
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Current Anthropic support is API key plus Claude CLI, not Agent SDK:** docs/providers/anthropic.md documents only API key and Claude CLI auth routes. A repo-wide search for '@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk' / 'claude-agent-sdk' in package.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, src, docs, and extensions returned no matches on main.
+  - file: [docs/providers/anthropic.md:10](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/providers/anthropic.md#L10)
+  - command: `rg -n "@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk|claude-agent-sdk" package.json pnpm-lock.yaml extensions src docs`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- This closes a core feature request on architectural-scope grounds; maintainers could still choose to revisit that roadmap guardrail later if product direction changes.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing this as better suited for [ClawHub](https://clawhub.ai/)/community plugin work after Codex review.
+

+## Decision
+
+Close: belongs on ClawHub
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Issue #10161 asks for a new external skill/workflow integration (`service-ability-creator`) in core. Current `main` explicitly directs new skills and community capabilities to ClawHub instead of OpenClaw core, and the repo already documents and implements ClawHub-backed skill/plugin installation plus workspace skill generation.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Vision says new skills belong on ClawHub, not core:** The project vision states that new skills should be published to ClawHub first and lists 'New core skills when they can live on ClawHub' under items the project will not merge for now.
+  - file: [VISION.md:69](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/VISION.md#L69)
+  - command: `nl -ba VISION.md | sed -n '60,110p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **ClawHub is the documented registry for skills and plugins:** The ClawHub docs describe it as the public registry for OpenClaw skills and plugins, with native `openclaw skills install <skill-slug>` and `openclaw plugins install clawhub:<package>` flows, plus publishing of new skills.
+  - file: [docs/tools/clawhub.md:10](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/tools/clawhub.md#L10)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/tools/clawhub.md | sed -n '1,120p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Community extensions are expected to publish externally:** Community plugins are documented as third-party packages published on ClawHub or npm, and the docs explicitly say to publish on ClawHub instead of seeking repo-level discoverability changes.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/community.md:9](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/plugins/community.md#L9)

+  - file: [docs/automation/cron-jobs.md:12](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/automation/cron-jobs.md#L12)

+## Decision

+Close: already implemented on main

+Confidence: high

+Action taken: proposed_close

+## Summary
+
+Current main already handles provider 429s with cooldown/backoff, auth-profile rotation, and configured model fallback, and the same behavior is present in release v2026.4.22.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Rate-limit escalation to fallback:** The runner tracks rate-limit profile rotations and, once the configured cap is exceeded, throws a FailoverError to move to the next fallback model/provider.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts:493](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts#L493)

+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Exponential cooldown with model scope:** Auth-profile failures apply cooldown backoff and preserve model-scoped rate-limit metadata so sibling models can still be tried when appropriate.
+  - file: [src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts:749](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts#L749)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts | sed -n '720,790p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Fallback engine skips/probes cooldowned candidates:** Model fallback walks the candidate chain, skips fully cooldowned providers when needed, and can probe transient cooldown candidates without blocking cross-provider failover.
+  - file: [src/agents/model-fallback.ts:678](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/agents/model-fallback.ts#L678)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/model-fallback.ts | sed -n '678,860p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Retry-After handling for SDK-managed 429s:** Provider transport parses retry-after headers and disables long SDK-internal retries so OpenClaw can surface the 429 and run failover logic instead of sleeping inside the SDK.
+  - file: [src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts:11](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts#L11)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts | sed -n '1,240p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Docs and tests match the requested behavior:** Docs describe model-scoped rate-limit cooldowns, exponential backoff, and switching to configured fallback models after one same-provider retry; e2e tests verify cross-provider fallback on rate limits and the configurable rotation cap.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/model-failover.md:149](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/docs/concepts/model-failover.md#L149)

+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+

+- The issue body also mentions optional request batching/coalescing; that specific idea is not what I relied on for closure. The close is based on the main reported need—429 backoff/cooldown plus fallback/load-balancing behavior—already being implemented.

+# [#10242: [Feature Request] Restore "New Thread" capability for Feishu (Lark) Channel in DMs](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10242)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10242](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10242)
+
+Author: Zebra-GPT
+
+Author association: NONE
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 6, 2026, 07:47 UTC
+
+Updated at: Feb 6, 2026, 07:47 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [cbe5515b70ab](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cbe5515b70abaeba98550b58d739ca0896907c43)
+
+Codex review: model gpt-5.5, reasoning high
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.24](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.24) ([cbcfdf62c729](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cbcfdf62c7297bda66009ea7476f053c3e9addab))
+
+Fixed in: commit [cbe5515b70ab](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cbe5515b70abaeba98550b58d739ca0896907c43)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main implements the requested Feishu/Lark behavior: the channel advertises thread support, ordinary sends can go out without reply metadata as new top-level messages, and explicit thread replies use Feishu reply-in-thread semantics. The remaining proof is source/test evidence; no live Feishu send was performed in this read-only review.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Feishu channel capabilities:** The Feishu plugin now advertises direct/channel chat types with `threads: true`, plus reply/media support.
+  - file: [extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts:586](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cbe5515b70abaeba98550b58d739ca0896907c43/extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts#L586)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts | sed -n '520,780p'`
+  - sha: [cbe5515b70ab](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cbe5515b70abaeba98550b58d739ca0896907c43)
+- **Message action routing:** The Feishu action handler distinguishes `send` from `thread-reply`: `send` leaves `replyToMessageId` undefined, while `thread-reply` requires `messageId` and passes `replyInThread: true`.
+  - file: [extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts:695](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cbe5515b70abaeba98550b58d739ca0896907c43/extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts#L695)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts | sed -n '520,780p'`
+  - sha: [cbe5515b70ab](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cbe5515b70abaeba98550b58d739ca0896907c43)
+- **Top-level Feishu send path:** `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect` calls direct `im.message.create` when no `replyToMessageId` exists, and only uses `im.message.reply` with optional `reply_in_thread` when a reply target exists.
+  - file: [extensions/feishu/src/send.ts:143](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cbe5515b70abaeba98550b58d739ca0896907c43/extensions/feishu/src/send.ts#L143)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/feishu/src/send.ts | sed -n '430,486p'`
+  - sha: [cbe5515b70ab](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cbe5515b70abaeba98550b58d739ca0896907c43)
+- **Outbound reply target handling:** The outbound adapter derives a reply target only from `replyToId` or `threadId`; if both are absent, the send path receives no `replyToMessageId`.
+  - file: [extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts:65](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cbe5515b70abaeba98550b58d739ca0896907c43/extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts#L65)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts | sed -n '1,320p'`

+# [#10268: Support Copilot hosted tools (web_search_preview) for free Bing search](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10268)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10268](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10268)
+
+Author: ChristianFJung
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 6, 2026, 08:33 UTC
+
+Updated at: Feb 10, 2026, 07:24 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: cannot reproduce on current main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+The issue’s core claim no longer holds: the reporter later verified that GitHub Copilot’s Chat Completions endpoint silently ignores `web_search_options`, so there is no reproducible Copilot-hosted Bing search capability for OpenClaw to wire up on current main. Code inspection also shows native `web_search` injection exists only for OpenAI/OpenAI Codex paths, while the GitHub Copilot wrapper only adjusts headers/payload rewriting and does not expose a hosted-search hook.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Reporter invalidated the original premise:** The follow-up issue comment says live Copilot testing with debug instrumentation showed `web_search_options` is silently ignored, with no annotations or web-search indicators returned.
+- **Copilot provider wrapper has no hosted-search injection path:** The GitHub Copilot stream wrapper only adds Copilot headers for Anthropic transport and rewrites payload connection-bound IDs for Responses transport; it does not add `web_search_options` or a native `web_search` tool.
+  - file: [extensions/github-copilot/stream.ts:52](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/extensions/github-copilot/stream.ts#L52)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/github-copilot/stream.ts | sed -n '1,220p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **OpenAI native web search is intentionally limited to OpenAI provider models:** `shouldEnableOpenAINativeWebSearch` only activates when the provider normalizes to `openai` and the model uses the OpenAI Responses API on an OpenAI base URL.
+  - file: [extensions/openai/native-web-search.ts:19](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/extensions/openai/native-web-search.ts#L19)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/openai/native-web-search.ts | sed -n '1,220p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Codex native search is intentionally limited to Codex-capable models:** Codex native web search eligibility is restricted to `openai-codex` or `openai-codex-responses`, not `github-copilot`.
+  - file: [src/agents/codex-native-web-search.ts:34](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/agents/codex-native-web-search.ts#L34)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/codex-native-web-search.ts | sed -n '1,240p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- GitHub could add supported Copilot-side hosted web search later, which would justify a fresh issue with the new API contract and repro.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing this as not reproducible on current `main` after Codex review.
+
+The issue’s core claim no longer holds: the reporter later verified that GitHub Copilot’s Chat Completions endpoint silently ignores `web_search_options`, so there is no reproducible Copilot-hosted Bing search capability for OpenClaw to wire up on current main. Code inspection also shows native `web_search` injection exists only for OpenAI/OpenAI Codex paths, while the GitHub Copilot wrapper only adjusts headers/payload rewriting and does not expose a hosted-search hook.
+

+
+- **Reporter invalidated the original premise:** The follow-up issue comment says live Copilot testing with debug instrumentation showed `web_search_options` is silently ignored, with no annotations or web-search indicators returned.
+- **Copilot provider wrapper has no hosted-search injection path:** The GitHub Copilot stream wrapper only adds Copilot headers for Anthropic transport and rewrites payload connection-bound IDs for Responses transport; it does not add `web_search_options` or a native `web_search` tool. ([`extensions/github-copilot/stream.ts:52`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/extensions/github-copilot/stream.ts#L52), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+- **OpenAI native web search is intentionally limited to OpenAI provider models:** `shouldEnableOpenAINativeWebSearch` only activates when the provider normalizes to `openai` and the model uses the OpenAI Responses API on an OpenAI base URL. ([`extensions/openai/native-web-search.ts:19`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/extensions/openai/native-web-search.ts#L19), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))

+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main already handles Android `SpeechRecognizer.ERROR_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS` on the active microphone capture path, and the issue’s cited legacy recognizer paths are not the shipped Android flow anymore.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Active Android voice path is `MicCaptureManager`:** `NodeRuntime` constructs `MicCaptureManager`, exposes all `mic*` state from it, and enables it from `prefs.talkEnabled`, so the Voice tab STT loop runs through this manager rather than `TalkModeManager` or `VoiceWakeManager`.
+  - file: [apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/NodeRuntime.kt:363](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/NodeRuntime.kt#L363)
+  - command: `nl -ba apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/NodeRuntime.kt | sed -n '363,417p'; nl -ba apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/NodeRuntime.kt | sed -n '602,610p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Voice UI is wired to the mic-capture state:** `MainViewModel` publishes `micStatusText`, `micIsListening`, and related state from the runtime mic fields, and `VoiceTabScreen` consumes those `mic*` flows.
+  - file: [apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/MainViewModel.kt:104](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/MainViewModel.kt#L104)
+  - command: `nl -ba apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/MainViewModel.kt | sed -n '104,113p'; nl -ba apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/ui/VoiceTabScreen.kt | sed -n '83,92p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **`ERROR_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS` is already handled with custom UX and longer backoff:** `MicCaptureManager` maps `SpeechRecognizer.ERROR_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS` to `"Speech requests limited; retrying"` and uses a `2_500L` restart delay instead of the generic 600 ms retry.
+  - file: [apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/voice/MicCaptureManager.kt:636](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/voice/MicCaptureManager.kt#L636)
+  - command: `nl -ba apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/voice/MicCaptureManager.kt | sed -n '623,658p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Legacy voice-wake recognizer path is disabled on startup:** `NodeRuntime` forces `voiceWakeMode` to `Off` during initialization, so `VoiceWakeManager` is not an active shipped path.
+  - file: [apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/NodeRuntime.kt:593](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/NodeRuntime.kt#L593)
+  - command: `nl -ba apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/NodeRuntime.kt | sed -n '593,596p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **`TalkModeManager` recognizer enable path has no current call sites:** Repo search found no `talkMode.setEnabled(...)` or `voiceReplySpeaker.setEnabled(...)` usages on main, which means the issue’s `TalkModeManager` STT error branch is not the active Android capture flow.
+  - command: `rg -n "setEnabled\(|talkMode\.setEnabled|voiceReplySpeaker\.setEnabled|TalkModeManager\(" apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **The behavior is present in the latest shipped release:** The `v2026.4.22` release commit contains the same `MicCaptureManager` `ERROR_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS` handling and the same startup `voiceWakeMode = Off` logic.
+  - file: [apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/voice/MicCaptureManager.kt:636](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/voice/MicCaptureManager.kt#L636)
+  - command: `git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/voice/MicCaptureManager.kt | nl -ba | sed -n '623,658p'; git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/NodeRuntime.kt | nl -ba | sed -n '593,606p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+

+- If Android re-enables `VoiceWakeManager` or starts using `TalkModeManager.setEnabled(...)` again, those legacy recognizer branches still only have generic `else -> "Speech error ($error)"` handling and would need parity with `MicCaptureManager`.

+- **Docs describe keyword-only fallback:** Builtin memory docs state that without an embedding provider, keyword search remains available, and the engine provides FTS5/BM25 keyword search.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md:14](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md#L14)
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Local embedding provider exists in code:** `createLocalEmbeddingProvider()` loads a local GGUF embedding model through `node-llama-cpp` and exposes `embedQuery`/`embedBatch` without any external API dependency.
+  - file: [src/memory-host-sdk/host/embeddings.ts:23](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/memory-host-sdk/host/embeddings.ts#L23)
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Provider-less FTS/BM25 search path exists:** The memory manager has an explicit `FTS-only mode: no embedding provider available` branch that runs keyword search, expands conversational queries into keywords, merges results, and returns scored results even with no provider.
+  - file: [extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts:341](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts#L341)

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already implements direct HTTP hook ingress for agent runs. OpenClaw exposes `POST /hooks/agent`, accepts agent/task fields including `message`, `agentId`, `sessionKey`, delivery options, and model overrides, then dispatches the run and returns a `runId`. The behavior is documented and covered by gateway hook tests, so the feature request is already satisfied on `main` and in the latest release evidence checked.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Direct HTTP agent hook is implemented in gateway server:** `/hooks/agent` validates the payload, enforces agent/session policy, dispatches the agent hook, and returns `{ ok: true, runId }`.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-http.ts:663](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/gateway/server-http.ts#L663)

+- **Docs advertise the exact endpoint and payload surface:** Gateway config reference documents `POST /hooks/agent` with `message`, `name`, `agentId`, `sessionKey`, `deliver`, `channel`, `to`, `model`, `thinking`, and `timeoutSeconds`, plus session-key policy notes.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md:3258](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md#L3258)

+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Tests cover end-to-end hook-triggered agent execution:** Gateway hook tests post to `/hooks/agent`, verify successful execution, external content source tagging, model override handling, and explicit `agentId` routing.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts:152](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts#L152)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts | sed -n '148,221p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Tests cover session-key policy and direct request session routing:** Tests verify default hook session keys, allowed templated mapping keys, and rejection of disallowed request/mapped session keys for `/hooks/agent` and mapped hooks.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts:361](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts#L361)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts | sed -n '358,430p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Tests cover run tracking via returned runId and idempotent retries:** Repeated `/hooks/agent` deliveries with the same idempotency key return the same `runId`, which satisfies the request to return a task/job identifier for tracking.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts:571](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts#L571)

+- **Latest release contains the same hook endpoint/docs:** The latest release commit still contains the implemented `/hooks/agent` route and published configuration-reference docs for it, so this was already shipped in the tagged release checked.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-http.ts:663](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/gateway/server-http.ts#L663)
+  - command: `git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:src/gateway/server-http.ts | sed -n '650,760p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)

+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- A follow-up comment asked for HMAC signature support for GitHub webhooks, but current evidence only shows bearer-token-based hook auth; that is a separate enhancement from the direct-trigger feature requested here.

+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10485](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10485)
+
+Author: heybeaux
+

+
+Labels: enhancement

+Created at: Feb 6, 2026, 15:24 UTC
+
+Updated at: Feb 10, 2026, 07:22 UTC

+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main now strips lightweight Markdown from auto-TTS text before speech provider synthesis, covering the reported spoken asterisks/underscores/backticks/header/blockquote problem.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Auto-TTS pipeline strips before synthesis:** `maybeApplyTtsToPayload` normalizes `textForAudio` with `stripMarkdown(textForAudio).trim()` immediately before calling `textToSpeech` with that cleaned text.
+  - file: [extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts:1174](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts#L1174)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts | sed -n '1040,1208p'`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Speech providers receive the cleaned text:** `textToSpeech` delegates to `synthesizeSpeech`, and `synthesizeSpeech` passes `params.text` into the selected provider's `synthesize` call, so the cleaned auto-TTS text is what providers receive.
+  - file: [extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts:769](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts#L769)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts | sed -n '760,900p'`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Markdown cleaner covers the reported formatting symbols:** The shared `stripMarkdown` helper removes bold/italic markers, headings, blockquotes, horizontal rules, inline code markers, excess blank lines, and trims the result.
+  - file: [src/shared/text/strip-markdown.ts:5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/src/shared/text/strip-markdown.ts#L5)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/shared/text/strip-markdown.ts | sed -n '1,260p'`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Regression coverage exists:** The TTS text preparation tests cover headers, bold/italic markers, underscores inside words, inline code, blockquotes, horizontal rules, and mixed Markdown replies.
+  - file: [src/tts/prepare-text.test.ts:12](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/src/tts/prepare-text.test.ts#L12)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/tts/prepare-text.test.ts | sed -n '1,220p'`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Fix/proving commit identified:** `git blame` attributes the stripping helper, tests, and TTS pipeline call to commit `607bc53ff38c9ab2e83495bf57247bc63541d0eb`. No release tag in this checkout contains that commit, so no fixed release was inferred.
+  - file: [extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts:1174](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/607bc53ff38c9ab2e83495bf57247bc63541d0eb/extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts#L1174)
+  - command: `git blame -L 1168,1182 -- extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts`
+  - sha: [607bc53ff38c](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/607bc53ff38c9ab2e83495bf57247bc63541d0eb)
+- **Issue is eligible for maintainer cleanup:** The GitHub context shows author association `NONE`, only the `enhancement` label, no protected labels, and related #12232 was closed after cross-referencing this issue.
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Tests were not executed in this read-only review; `pnpm` is unavailable in the environment and the task prohibits artifact-generating validation. Source and committed test coverage were inspected instead.
+- The implementation is always-on for auto-TTS rather than a user-facing `messages.tts.stripMarkdown` toggle; it still solves the reported raw-Markdown speech problem on current main.
+

+
+Closing this as implemented after Codex automated review.
+
+Current main now strips lightweight Markdown from auto-TTS text before speech provider synthesis, covering the reported spoken asterisks/underscores/backticks/header/blockquote problem.
+
+What I checked:
+
+- **Auto-TTS pipeline strips before synthesis:** `maybeApplyTtsToPayload` normalizes `textForAudio` with `stripMarkdown(textForAudio).trim()` immediately before calling `textToSpeech` with that cleaned text. ([`extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts:1174`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711d/extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts#L1174), [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711d))
+- **Speech providers receive the cleaned text:** `textToSpeech` delegates to `synthesizeSpeech`, and `synthesizeSpeech` passes `params.text` into the selected provider's `synthesize` call, so the cleaned auto-TTS text is what providers receive. ([`extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts:769`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711d/extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts#L769), [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711d))
+- **Markdown cleaner covers the reported formatting symbols:** The shared `stripMarkdown` helper removes bold/italic markers, headings, blockquotes, horizontal rules, inline code markers, excess blank lines, and trims the result. ([`src/shared/text/strip-markdown.ts:5`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711d/src/shared/text/strip-markdown.ts#L5), [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711d))
+- **Regression coverage exists:** The TTS text preparation tests cover headers, bold/italic markers, underscores inside words, inline code, blockquotes, horizontal rules, and mixed Markdown replies. ([`src/tts/prepare-text.test.ts:12`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711d/src/tts/prepare-text.test.ts#L12), [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711d))
+- **Fix/proving commit identified:** `git blame` attributes the stripping helper, tests, and TTS pipeline call to commit `607bc53ff38c9ab2e83495bf57247bc63541d0eb`. No release tag in this checkout contains that commit, so no fixed release was inferred. ([`extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts:1174`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/607bc53ff38c/extensions/speech-core/src/tts.ts#L1174), [607bc53ff38c](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/607bc53ff38c))
+- **Issue is eligible for maintainer cleanup:** The GitHub context shows author association `NONE`, only the `enhancement` label, no protected labels, and related #12232 was closed after cross-referencing this issue.
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.

+## Summary
+
+Close as `clawhub`. Current main keeps internal workspace hooks limited to command/message/session/gateway events, while the existing plugin hook surface already exposes tool lifecycle interception and approval gating. The request is useful, but it fits a plugin/ClawHub implementation rather than expanding OpenClaw core internal hooks.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Internal hooks are intentionally narrower:** The internal hooks docs describe them as small gateway scripts for command and lifecycle events, and the published event list does not include tool-call/tool-result events.
+  - file: [docs/automation/hooks.md:11](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/automation/hooks.md#L11)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/automation/hooks.md | sed -n '11,50p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Plugin hooks already expose tool lifecycle data:** The plugin hook contract includes `before_tool_call` with tool name and params, `after_tool_call` with tool name, params, result, error, and `durationMs`, plus `tool_result_persist` for persisted tool-result handling.
+  - file: [src/plugins/hook-types.ts:306](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/plugins/hook-types.ts#L306)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/hook-types.ts | sed -n '306,365p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Core runtime executes those plugin tool hooks:** The hook runner has dedicated `runBeforeToolCall`, `runAfterToolCall`, and `runToolResultPersist` paths.
+  - file: [src/plugins/hooks.ts:777](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/plugins/hooks.ts#L777)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/hooks.ts | sed -n '777,845p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Approval gating use case is already covered on the plugin surface:** Plugin docs state `before_tool_call` can return `requireApproval`, which pauses agent execution and routes approval through the existing approval UX. Tests also cover blocking tool execution via `before_tool_call` on HTTP and MCP tool surfaces.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:184](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/plugins/building-plugins.md#L184)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/building-plugins.md | sed -n '182,194p'; nl -ba src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts | sed -n '441,460p'; nl -ba src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts | sed -n '92,130p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Project vision prefers optional capability as plugins:** VISION says OpenClaw has an extensive plugin API, core should stay lean, and optional capability should usually ship as plugins.
+  - file: [VISION.md:52](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/VISION.md#L52)
+  - command: `nl -ba VISION.md | sed -n '52,60p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Users who specifically want file-based internal hook scripts, not plugins, still do not have a first-class tool lifecycle event surface there.
+- If maintainers later decide internal hooks should gain parity with plugin hooks for operator scripting, this issue may need to be reopened or superseded.
+
+## Close Comment

+

The request is useful, but it fits a plugin/ClawHub implementation rather than expanding OpenClaw core internal hooks.

Public docs: [`docs/automation/hooks.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/hooks). ([`docs/automation/hooks.md:11`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/hooks), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+- **Plugin hooks already expose tool lifecycle data:** The plugin hook contract includes `before_tool_call` with tool name and params, `after_tool_call` with tool name, params, result, error, and `durationMs`, plus `tool_result_persist` for persisted tool-result handling. +- **Core runtime executes those plugin tool hooks:** The hook runner has dedicated `runBeforeToolCall`, `runAfterToolCall`, and `runToolResultPersist` paths. ([`src/plugins/hooks.ts:777`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe/src/plugins/hooks.ts#L777), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))
+- **Approval gating use case is already covered on the plugin surface:** Plugin docs state `before_tool_call` can return `requireApproval`, which pauses agent execution and routes approval through the existing approval UX. Tests also cover blocking tool execution via `before_tool_call` on HTTP and MCP tool surfaces. Public docs: [`docs/plugins/building-plugins.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/building-plugins). ([`docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:184`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/building-plugins), [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe))

+## Summary
+
+Current main already has a native post-compaction continuity path: OpenClaw injects a post-compaction system refresh for the next turn, re-injects configured AGENTS.md sections, and runs an automatic pre-compaction memory flush to preserve durable context.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Core enqueues a post-compaction system event:** After compaction, the reply runner calls readPostCompactionContext(...) and enqueues the returned content as a system event for the session.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts:1572](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts#L1572)
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Injected payload is a dedicated post-compaction refresh:** The post-compaction helper reads workspace AGENTS.md, extracts configured sections, substitutes YYYY-MM-DD with the real date, and returns a '[Post-compaction context refresh]' prompt.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/post-compaction-context.ts:71](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/auto-reply/reply/post-compaction-context.ts#L71)
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **User-facing config documents the feature:** Config docs expose agents.defaults.compaction.postCompactionSections for post-compaction reinjection and document the built-in memoryFlush step before compaction.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md:1416](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md#L1416)
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Docs describe automatic continuity preservation:** Memory docs state that before compaction OpenClaw runs a silent turn that saves important context to memory files to prevent context loss.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/memory.md:105](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/docs/concepts/memory.md#L105)
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Tests cover the recovery behavior:** Tests verify that followup runs receive the post-compaction refresh prompt and that extracted sections can point the agent at dated memory files after YYYY-MM-DD substitution.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts:858](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts#L858)
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions

+- The exact injectFile/onCompaction config shape proposed in the issue is not present; current core solves the continuity problem via AGENTS.md section reinjection plus memory flush instead of arbitrary file-content injection.

+- **Context engine plugin slot covers custom recall and compaction:** The docs describe context engines as the component that controls context assembly, summarization, and cross-session recall, and note plugin engines can implement their own compaction strategy and background post-turn work.

+- **Safe session transcript mutation API exists:** `ContextEngineRuntimeContext` exposes `rewriteTranscriptEntries`, and the runtime implementation rewrites transcript entries by branching and re-appending safely under a session write lock.

+  - command: `nl -ba src/context-engine/types.ts | sed -n '1,240p'`
+- **Deferred background transcript maintenance is implemented:** Turn maintenance can be deferred to a background lane, wait for the session lane to go idle, then execute transcript maintenance and report rewritten entries/bytes freed.

+  - command: `nl -ba docs/concepts/memory.md | sed -n '1,220p'`

+Close: belongs on ClawHub

+Confidence: high

+Action taken: proposed_close

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` does not add a core `humanDelay.mode = "proportional"`; core still only supports `off`, `natural`, and `custom`. But this request now fits the plugin surface: OpenClaw’s scope guidance says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and current outbound delivery exposes an async `message_sending` hook that can inspect outgoing content and delay before send. That makes proportional pacing a better ClawHub plugin than a new core config mode.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Scope guidance favors plugins for optional capability:** `VISION.md:54-59` says OpenClaw has an extensive plugin API, core should stay lean, and optional capability should usually ship as plugins.
+  - file: [VISION.md:54](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/VISION.md#L54)
+  - command: `nl -ba VISION.md | sed -n '50,72p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Plugin SDK documents outbound hook for message sending:** `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md:212-215` documents the `message_sending` hook and its decision semantics for outbound messages.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md:212](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md#L212)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md | sed -n '204,220p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Hook runner is async and sequential:** `src/plugins/hooks.ts:733-741` states `runMessageSending` runs sequentially and allows plugins to modify or cancel outgoing messages.
+  - file: [src/plugins/hooks.ts:733](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/plugins/hooks.ts#L733)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/hooks.ts | sed -n '733,748p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Outbound delivery awaits the hook before sending:** `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts:567-607` awaits `runMessageSending(...)` before continuing, so a plugin can impose content-length-based delay prior to send.
+  - file: [src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts:567](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts#L567)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts | sed -n '567,660p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+- **Core humanDelay schema remains intentionally narrow:** `src/config/types.base.ts:94-100` and `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts:527-533` still define only `off | natural | custom` plus `minMs`/`maxMs`; there is no built-in `proportional` mode or `msPerChar` setting on `main`.
+  - file: [src/config/types.base.ts:94](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a/src/config/types.base.ts#L94)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/types.base.ts | sed -n '88,120p'; nl -ba src/config/zod-schema.core.ts | sed -n '520,550p'`
+  - sha: [754acc4478fe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/754acc4478fe361db1e5303fd60f589d52beea7a)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- There is no in-tree ClawHub/plugin implementation to point the reporter at yet; this decision is about scope, not availability of a ready-made package.
+- The current hook surface exposes content and conversation context but not an explicit block-vs-final reply kind, so a plugin would need its own pacing heuristics or per-conversation state if it wants to mimic core `humanDelay` semantics exactly.

+## Decision

+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close

+## Summary
+
+Current main already implements auto-compaction near the effective context limit, includes the optional pre-compaction memory flush requested here, and exposes threshold-equivalent tuning through `agents.defaults.compaction.reserveTokensFloor`, `agents.defaults.compaction.memoryFlush.softThresholdTokens`, and runtime `contextTokens` caps.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Feature shipped in changelog:** `CHANGELOG.md` records the feature in release `2026.1.11`: automatic pre-compaction memory flush, reserved compaction headroom, and config for `agents.defaults.compaction.*`.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:6473](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/CHANGELOG.md#L6473)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '6468,6512p'`
+- **Docs say auto-compaction is default-on:** The user docs state that auto-compaction is on by default, runs when the session nears the context limit or overflows, and can run a silent memory flush before compaction.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/compaction.md:27](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/concepts/compaction.md#L27)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/concepts/compaction.md | sed -n '1,260p'`
+- **Threshold semantics are already configurable:** The session-management deep dive documents Pi's threshold rule as `contextTokens > contextWindow - reserveTokens`, and documents OpenClaw's configurable safety floor via `agents.defaults.compaction.reserveTokensFloor` plus the pre-threshold memory flush.
+  - file: [docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md:228](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md#L228)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md | sed -n '220,355p'`
+- **Config surface exists on main:** `agents.defaults.compaction` already exposes `reserveTokensFloor`, `notifyUser`, `model`, `provider`, and `memoryFlush.softThresholdTokens` in the config reference.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md:1403](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md#L1403)
+  - command: `sed -n '1385,1455p' docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`
+- **Current code computes and triggers the threshold:** `agent-runner-memory.ts` computes `threshold = contextWindowTokens - reserveTokensFloor - softThresholdTokens` and triggers preflight compaction when projected usage crosses it.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts:392](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts#L392)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts | sed -n '388,460p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Effective context cap is honored, including small-model fixes:** Current main applies the effective runtime context cap to Pi's auto-compaction threshold (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts`), resolves configured `contextTokens` as the effective limit (`src/agents/context.ts`), and has regression coverage for small-context reserve-floor behavior (`src/agents/pi-settings.test.ts`).
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts:479](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts#L479)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts | sed -n '470,520p'; nl -ba src/agents/context.ts | sed -n '66,90p'; nl -ba src/agents/pi-settings.test.ts | sed -n '189,272p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Checkout stayed clean:** Read-only inspection left the repository clean; `git status --porcelain=v1` returned no output.
+  - command: `git status --porcelain=v1`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions

+## Summary

+Current `main` already implements restart/reconnect resume handling for session-scoped gateway restarts via the restart-sentinel path, including waking the agent, preserving routing/thread context, and dispatching a continuation turn back into the same session.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Restart sentinel now wakes the session immediately:** `enqueueRestartSentinelWake()` injects a system event for the session and then calls `requestHeartbeatNow({ reason: "wake", sessionKey })`.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.ts:45](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.ts#L45)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.ts | sed -n '45,60p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Startup schedules restart-sentinel recovery after gateway attach:** Gateway startup checks for a persisted restart sentinel and schedules `scheduleRestartSentinelWake()` on boot, covering gateway restart/reconnect recovery.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts:338](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts#L338)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts | sed -n '338,349p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Continuation is routed back into the same session/thread:** After loading the sentinel, current `main` resolves delivery context, sends the restart notice, and then dispatches the sentinel continuation via `recordInboundSessionAndDispatchReply(...)` using the original session/channel/thread route.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.ts:325](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.ts#L325)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.ts | sed -n '325,443p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Default restart continuation explicitly resumes pending work:** `buildRestartSuccessContinuation()` defaults to an `agentTurn` message telling the agent to inform the user the gateway restarted successfully and continue any pending work.
+  - file: [src/infra/restart-sentinel.ts:65](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/infra/restart-sentinel.ts#L65)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/restart-sentinel.ts | sed -n '65,67p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Regression-proof tests cover wake and continuation behavior:** Tests assert that restart recovery enqueues the restart message, calls `requestHeartbeatNow`, and dispatches the continuation back into the same routed thread/session.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.test.ts:217](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.test.ts#L217)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.test.ts | sed -n '217,245p'; nl -ba src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.test.ts | sed -n '383,435p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Implementation landed on main after the latest release:** The restart-sentinel recovery files were introduced in commit `37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096`, which is newer than the provided latest release `v2026.4.22`.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096/src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.ts#L1)
+  - command: `git show --stat --summary 37c25069 -- src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.ts src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts src/infra/restart-sentinel.ts src/infra/restart-sentinel.test.ts`
+  - sha: [37c250695b60](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions

+- This evidence is strongest for gateway restart/reboot recovery through the restart-sentinel path; the original issue body also mentioned generic network hiccups, but the maintainer note linked this report to the restart-sentinel regression and current `main` now implements that recovery path.

+## Decision

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already supports spawning external harness/CLI agents through `sessions_spawn` via the ACP runtime, including Codex and other supported CLIs, with agent config for ACP defaults and background task tracking.
+
+## Evidence

+# [#10841: Reminders set for wrong times because agent doesn't know current time](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10841)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10841](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10841)

+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
