+## Summary
+
+Keep open. Current `main` has partial mitigations for time awareness, but there is not strong evidence that issue #10841 is fixed: `session_status` still does not pass a current-time line or machine-readable current timestamp into the status card, so the report's core complaint remains plausible.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **`session_status` does not supply current time:** `src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts:551-599` calls `buildStatusText(...)` with model/task/session fields only. It does not pass `timeLine`, epoch, ISO time, or similar current-time data before returning `statusText`.
+  - file: [src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts:551](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts#L551)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts | sed -n '560,640p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Status card only renders time when caller provides `args.timeLine`:** `src/status/status-message.ts:881-899` includes `args.timeLine` in the output array, but nothing in the `session_status` call path above sets it.
+  - file: [src/status/status-message.ts:881](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/status/status-message.ts#L881)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/status/status-message.ts | sed -n '876,902p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **A precise current-time helper exists, but is wired into cron rather than `session_status`:** `src/agents/current-time.ts:23-30` builds `Current time: <local> (<tz>) / <UTC>`, and cron appends it in `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts:580-613`. That helps cron turns, but it is separate from the `session_status` path named in the issue.
+  - file: [src/agents/current-time.ts:23](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/current-time.ts#L23)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/current-time.ts | sed -n '20,40p'; nl -ba src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts | sed -n '578,614p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Inbound message context now carries timestamps:** `src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.ts:194-216` injects `conversationInfo.timestamp`, and `src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts:372-396` verifies timestamp inclusion and user-timezone formatting. This is a mitigation, but it is not proof that reminder scheduling is fixed across flows.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.ts:194](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.ts#L194)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.ts | sed -n '176,240p'; nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts | sed -n '372,402p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Docs still claim `session_status` provides current time:** `docs/concepts/system-prompt.md:151-153`, `docs/date-time.md:75-76`, and `docs/concepts/session-tool.md:81-85` say `session_status` includes a timestamp/time line, which does not match the implementation inspected above.

+# [#10880: [Feature]: Support Intel (x86_64) Macs for the macOS desktop app](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10880)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10880](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10880)
+
+Author: Mokoby
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 7, 2026, 04:11 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 1, 2026, 21:48 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` and the shipped `v2026.4.22` release already default the macOS desktop app packaging to universal binaries (`arm64` + `x86_64`), which implements the requested Intel Mac support at the app packaging level.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Release packaging defaults to universal on main:** `scripts/package-mac-app.sh` sets release builds to `BUILD_ARCHS_VALUE="all"`, then expands that to `arm64 x86_64`, so release app bundles are built as universal binaries unless explicitly overridden.
+  - file: [scripts/package-mac-app.sh:24](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/scripts/package-mac-app.sh#L24)
+  - command: `nl -ba scripts/package-mac-app.sh | sed -n '20,36p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Distribution packaging explicitly targets Apple Silicon and Intel:** `scripts/package-mac-dist.sh` exports `BUILD_ARCHS=${BUILD_ARCHS:-all}` and documents that distribution builds support both Apple Silicon and Intel Macs.
+  - file: [scripts/package-mac-dist.sh:17](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/scripts/package-mac-dist.sh#L17)
+  - command: `nl -ba scripts/package-mac-dist.sh | sed -n '14,24p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Latest shipped release tag contains the universal-build logic:** The `v2026.4.22` tag contains the same universal packaging defaults in both macOS packaging scripts, so this was not just added after the latest release.
+  - file: [scripts/package-mac-app.sh:24](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/scripts/package-mac-app.sh#L24)
+  - command: `git show v2026.4.22:scripts/package-mac-app.sh | nl -ba | sed -n '20,36p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Shipped changelog records the macOS universal packaging change:** The changelog in the release tag includes entries for defaulting macOS distribution/release packaging to universal binaries.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:2763](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/CHANGELOG.md#L2763)
+  - command: `git show v2026.4.22:CHANGELOG.md | rg -n "macOS release packaging: default .*universal binaries|macOS: default distribution packaging to universal binaries"`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Current appcast points at the shipped macOS release line:** `appcast.xml` shows `2026.4.22` as the current shipped macOS app release, matching the tag inspected above.
+  - file: [appcast.xml:6](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/appcast.xml#L6)
+  - command: `nl -ba appcast.xml | sed -n '1,20p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- This closes the packaging/support request for the desktop app itself. Separate Intel-specific limitations may still exist in individual native plugins or runtimes, but that is narrower than the app-binary support requested here.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
+
+Current `main` and the shipped `v2026.4.22` release already default the macOS desktop app packaging to universal binaries (`arm64` + `x86_64`), which implements the requested Intel Mac support at the app packaging level.

([`scripts/package-mac-app.sh:24`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06a/scripts/package-mac-app.sh#L24), [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))

([`scripts/package-mac-app.sh:24`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376/scripts/package-mac-app.sh#L24), [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376))

([`CHANGELOG.md:2763`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376/CHANGELOG.md#L2763), [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376))

+# [#10929: [Feature]: WhatsApp voice notes (OGG/Opus) fail transcription via Whisper, needs automatic transcoding to WAV](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10929)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10929](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/10929)
+
+Author: all4one-max
+

+

+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))

+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already handles the reported WhatsApp voice-note path: inbound WhatsApp audio defaults to `audio/ogg; codecs=opus`, saved media keeps a MIME-derived extension, and the media-understanding CLI path now transcodes non-`.wav` audio to mono 16 kHz PCM WAV before invoking `whisper-cli`. Regression tests cover both WhatsApp OGG/Opus MIME handling and OGG-to-WAV transcoding.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **WhatsApp voice notes default to OGG/Opus MIME:** Inbound WhatsApp audio falls back to `audio/ogg; codecs=opus` when Baileys omits the MIME, matching WhatsApp PTT voice-note format.
+  - file: [extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/media.ts:15](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/media.ts#L15)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/media.ts | sed -n '1,120p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Saved inbound media keeps MIME-derived extension:** `saveMediaBuffer` derives the saved file extension from the MIME header, so WhatsApp OGG/Opus audio is persisted with an audio extension instead of a bare filename.
+  - file: [src/media/store.ts:420](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/media/store.ts#L420)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/media/store.ts | sed -n '417,425p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Whisper CLI path now auto-transcodes non-WAV audio:** The media-understanding CLI runner converts any non-`.wav` audio input to mono 16 kHz PCM WAV before invoking `whisper-cli`, which addresses the reported OGG/Opus decode failure path.
+  - file: [src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts:220](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096/src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts#L220)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts | sed -n '220,260p'`
+  - sha: [37c250695b60](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096)
+- **Regression tests cover WhatsApp MIME handling:** There is a direct test that parameterized WhatsApp `Audio/Ogg; codecs=opus` input transcribes successfully through media understanding.
+  - file: [src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts:428](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096/src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts#L428)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts | sed -n '428,460p'`
+  - sha: [37c250695b60](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096)
+- **Regression tests cover OGG-to-WAV transcoding before whisper-cli:** There is a direct test asserting non-WAV audio is transcoded with ffmpeg and that `whisper-cli` receives the resulting `.wav` path.
+  - file: [src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts:714](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096/src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts#L714)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts | sed -n '714,772p'`
+  - sha: [37c250695b60](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The fix appears to be on `main` but not yet in a tagged release; `git tag --contains 37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096` returned no release tag, so users on `v2026.4.22` may still hit the old behavior.
+
+## Close Comment

Regression tests cover both WhatsApp OGG/Opus MIME handling and OGG-to-WAV transcoding.

+- **WhatsApp voice notes default to OGG/Opus MIME:** Inbound WhatsApp audio falls back to `audio/ogg; codecs=opus` when Baileys omits the MIME, matching WhatsApp PTT voice-note format. ([`extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/media.ts:15`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06a/extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/media.ts#L15), [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))

([`src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts:220`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/37c250695b60/src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts#L220), [37c250695b60](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/37c250695b60))

([`src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts:428`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/37c250695b60/src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts#L428), [37c250695b60](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/37c250695b60))

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already has a WhatsApp history-length setting. `channels.whatsapp.historyLimit` is part of the config contract, the WhatsApp runtime applies it when building injected group-history context, docs describe it as the supported knob, and schema/tests cover both root and per-account overrides.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **WhatsApp config exposes history limit:** `WhatsAppConfig` defines `historyLimit` as the max group messages kept as history context, plus `dmHistoryLimit` for DM turns.
+  - file: [src/config/types.whatsapp.ts:75](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/config/types.whatsapp.ts#L75)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/types.whatsapp.ts | sed -n '1,180p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Runtime uses the configured limit:** WhatsApp monitor resolves `groupHistoryLimit` from `account.historyLimit`, then `cfg.channels?.whatsapp?.historyLimit`, then `messages.groupChat.historyLimit`, before falling back to the default.
+  - file: [extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts:147](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts#L147)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts | sed -n '130,170p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Buffered history is injected as a bounded context window:** When processing a WhatsApp group message, the runtime converts only the retained history entries into the supplemental context block rather than loading unbounded history.
+  - file: [extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.ts:127](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.ts#L127)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.ts | sed -n '116,155p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Docs describe the supported knob:** WhatsApp docs explicitly document pending group history injection with default `50`, config key `channels.whatsapp.historyLimit`, fallback `messages.groupChat.historyLimit`, and `0` to disable.
+  - file: [docs/channels/whatsapp.md:255](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/channels/whatsapp.md#L255)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/channels/whatsapp.md | sed -n '200,320p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Schema/tests cover per-account override:** Config schema test accepts `historyLimit` at the channel root and inside `accounts.work.historyLimit`, confirming the setting is supported and validated.
+  - file: [extensions/whatsapp/src/config-schema.test.ts:42](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/extensions/whatsapp/src/config-schema.test.ts#L42)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/whatsapp/src/config-schema.test.ts | sed -n '1,80p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Introducing commit on main:** Commit `37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096` added the current WhatsApp plugin/docs/config surface, including the history-limit support now present on `main`.
+  - command: `git show --stat --summary 37c25069 -- extensions/whatsapp src/config/types.whatsapp.ts docs/channels/whatsapp.md`
+  - sha: [37c250695b60](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The current implementation clearly supports count-based history limits; the issue body also mentioned a possible age-based limit, which is not evidenced here.
+

+## Summary
+
+#11018 is a useful notification-router idea, but current main already exposes the needed plugin/event/task surfaces for this to live as a ClawHub plugin rather than a new native core system. The exact proposed native events_subscribe/policy JSON is not implemented, so this is not an implemented-on-main close.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Core scope favors plugins for optional capability:** VISION says core stays lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and code plugins are appropriate when runtime hooks, channels, tools, or in-process extension points are needed.
+  - file: [VISION.md:48](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/VISION.md#L48)
+  - command: `sed -n '1,220p' VISION.md`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Plugin hooks cover the relevant event sources:** The plugin hook catalog covers agent turns, tool calls, messages/delivery, session lifecycle, subagent routing/completion, and gateway lifecycle, which are the event sources requested for the notification router.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/hooks.md:55](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/docs/plugins/hooks.md#L55)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/hooks.md | sed -n '1,260p'`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Runtime helpers expose event subscriptions and session wake delivery:** Plugin runtime exposes system.enqueueSystemEvent, requestHeartbeatNow, and events.onAgentEvent/onSessionTranscriptUpdate, which can be used by a plugin to queue or wake proactive delivery without polling the model.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md:305](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md#L305)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md | sed -n '300,340p'`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Task notifications already cover push-driven completion/state changes:** Background tasks for ACP runs, subagents, cron, and CLI work are documented as push-driven, with direct channel delivery or session-queued fallback, and notify policies including done_only/state_changes/silent.
+  - file: [docs/automation/tasks.md:21](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/docs/automation/tasks.md#L21)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/automation/tasks.md | sed -n '1,170p'`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Proposed native agent tool is not present:** The proposed events_subscribe API is not present in current main, supporting clawhub/plugin-scope rather than implemented-on-main.
+  - command: `rg -n "events_subscribe" src docs ui apps package.json`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- A future maintainer may still want a first-party notification-router plugin or a smaller documented sample based on the linked userland implementation.
+- If a plugin author needs richer direct channel delivery than session-queued system events and existing reply/channel helpers provide, that should be tracked as a focused plugin API gap rather than this broad core feature.

+Type: issue

+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11025](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11025)

+Confidence: high

+Action taken: proposed_close

+## Summary
+
+Current main already auto-downscales/recompresses oversized images before provider calls, including browser screenshots, prompt/channel attachments, tool-result image blocks, and replayed session history. The same implementation is present in the `v2026.4.22` release tag for the relevant code paths.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Browser screenshots are normalized to the Anthropic-style 5 MB cap before being saved/returned:** `saveNormalizedScreenshotResponse()` runs `normalizeBrowserScreenshot()` with `DEFAULT_BROWSER_SCREENSHOT_MAX_BYTES = 5 * 1024 * 1024`, and the browser screenshot test asserts oversized images are reduced to `<=5MB` and `<=2000x2000`.
+  - file: [extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.snapshot.ts:139](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.snapshot.ts#L139)

+- **Generic tool image blocks are auto-sanitized before provider use:** `sanitizeContentBlocksImages()` recompresses/downscales oversized base64 images, targeting `DEFAULT_IMAGE_MAX_BYTES = 5 * 1024 * 1024`, and `imageResult()` routes tool outputs through `sanitizeToolResultImages()`.
+  - file: [src/agents/tool-images.ts:21](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/tool-images.ts#L21)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/tool-images.ts | sed -n '21,28p' ; nl -ba src/agents/tools/common.ts | sed -n '327,339p'`
+- **Prompt and channel attachment images are sanitized before the request is built:** `detectAndLoadPromptImages()` merges existing/offloaded attachment images and then calls `sanitizeImagesWithLog(..., "prompt:images")` before returning images for the model request.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.ts:555](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.ts#L555)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.ts | sed -n '555,569p'`
+- **Replayed session history is sanitized before provider calls:** `sanitizeSessionHistory()` calls `sanitizeSessionMessagesImages()` during replay, and the transcript hygiene doc explicitly says image payloads are always sanitized to prevent provider-side rejection due to size limits.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts:465](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts#L465)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts | sed -n '465,478p' ; nl -ba docs/reference/transcript-hygiene.md | sed -n '51,63p'`
+- **User-facing config/docs document the behavior:** `agents.defaults.imageMaxDimensionPx` is documented as controlling transcript/tool image downscaling before provider calls.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md:1125](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md#L1125)

+- **The released `v2026.4.22` tag already contains the same implementation files:** A scoped diff from `v2026.4.22` to `HEAD` for the implementation files was empty; only the transcript-hygiene doc text changed outside the code paths.

+# [#11133: Feature request: WebChat/Canvas image upload UI + unified attachment flow across channels](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11133)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11133](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11133)
+
+Author: antoniarco
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 7, 2026, 12:52 UTC
+
+Updated at: Feb 10, 2026, 07:15 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: commit [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already provides the requested browser chat image-attachment flow: the UI supports attach/paste/drag-drop with previews, the client sends images through `chat.send.attachments`, the gateway validates/sniffs/offloads images, and chat history reloads/rendering preserve uploaded and assistant-generated images via authenticated media routes.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Browser chat already supports attach, paste, drag-drop, and pre-send preview:** `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts` implements paste (`handlePaste`), file picker (`handleFileSelect`), drag-drop (`handleDrop`), preview/removal UI, and the paperclip/file input in the composer.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts:200](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts#L200)
+  - command: `nl -ba ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts | sed -n '196,322p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Composer sends images through `chat.send.attachments`:** `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts` converts browser data URLs to base64 attachment payloads, includes them in `chat.send`, and renders optimistic user-side image blocks immediately in the transcript.
+  - file: [ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts:213](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts#L213)
+  - command: `nl -ba ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts | sed -n '205,340p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Gateway already validates/sniffs/offloads image attachments:** `src/gateway/chat-attachments.ts` enforces base64 and size limits, sniffs MIME, drops non-images, logs mismatches, and offloads large/text-only-model images into managed media refs instead of losing them.
+  - file: [src/gateway/chat-attachments.ts:295](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/gateway/chat-attachments.ts#L295)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/chat-attachments.ts | sed -n '295,435p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **History reload and assistant image rendering are already covered by authenticated media handling:** `src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.ts` serves managed outgoing images behind gateway auth/session ownership checks, and `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts` renders those image attachments inline in chat.
+  - file: [src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.ts:976](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.ts#L976)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.ts | sed -n '976,1088p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Changelog explicitly records the user-facing pieces as delivered:** The changelog notes that user image uploads stay visible after reload, assistant-generated images persist as authenticated managed media, and WebChat preserves image attachments for text-only primary models.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/CHANGELOG.md#L22)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '20,60p' && nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '500,515p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The implementation does not match the issue's proposed design literally: current main uses direct `chat.send` attachment payloads and managed media routes rather than a separate Canvas-host `POST /assets` upload endpoint.
+- `src/gateway/protocol/schema/logs-chat.ts` still types `attachments` as `Type.Array(Type.Unknown())`, so the feature is implemented behaviorally even though one checklist item about formalizing the schema is not fully reflected there.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
+
+Current `main` already provides the requested browser chat image-attachment flow: the UI supports attach/paste/drag-drop with previews, the client sends images through `chat.send.attachments`, the gateway validates/sniffs/offloads images, and chat history reloads/rendering preserve uploaded and assistant-generated images via authenticated media routes.

([`src/gateway/chat-attachments.ts:295`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06a/src/gateway/chat-attachments.ts#L295), [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already supports Telegram delivery of new device-pairing requests through the bundled `device-pair` plugin, so the requested workflow exists even though it shipped as `/pair notify ...` rather than a `gateway.devices.notifyChannel` config key.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Telegram pairing notifications are implemented:** `handleNotifyCommand` exposes Telegram-only `on|off|once|status` controls, and `registerPairingNotifierService` polls pending pairing requests and sends Telegram messages with `/pair approve <requestId>` instructions.
+  - file: [extensions/device-pair/notify.ts:411](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/extensions/device-pair/notify.ts#L411)

+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **The `/pair` command wires the notify flow into Telegram pairing:** The bundled `device-pair` plugin registers `/pair`, routes `/pair notify ...`, and auto-arms a one-shot Telegram notification when `/pair qr` is used from Telegram.
+  - file: [extensions/device-pair/index.ts:547](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/extensions/device-pair/index.ts#L547)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/device-pair/index.ts | sed -n '540,660p'`

+  - file: [docs/tools/slash-commands.md:153](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/tools/slash-commands.md#L153)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/tools/slash-commands.md | sed -n '150,165p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Docs describe full Telegram pairing workflow:** Pairing docs say first-time device pairing can be done entirely from Telegram via the `device-pair` plugin.

+# [#11202: [Security] Model catalog with resolved apiKey values injected into LLM prompt context on every turn](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11202)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11202](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11202)
+
+Author: rplakas
+
+Labels: bug, security
+
+Created at: Feb 7, 2026, 14:51 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 29, 2026, 11:42 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` no longer carries resolved provider secrets through the model catalog or agent system prompt, and the remaining config-read surfaces redact provider `apiKey` values.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Model catalog shape is metadata-only:** `ModelCatalogEntry` contains only `id`, `name`, `provider`, `alias`, `contextWindow`, `reasoning`, and `input`, and `loadModelCatalog()` copies only those fields into the runtime catalog array.
+  - file: [src/agents/model-catalog.types.ts:3](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/model-catalog.types.ts#L3)
+- **Catalog loader strips provider secret fields:** The catalog loader iterates discovered entries and pushes only `{ id, name, provider, contextWindow, reasoning, input }`, so provider `apiKey`/header data is not retained in the prompt-visible catalog object.
+  - file: [src/agents/model-catalog.ts:141](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/model-catalog.ts#L141)
+- **System prompt gets aliases, not provider config:** Embedded runs pass `buildModelAliasLines(params.config)` into prompt construction; that helper emits only `- alias: provider/model`, and the system prompt renders only that alias section.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:914](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts#L914)
+- **models.json persistence now normalizes secrets to markers:** Provider normalization reverse-looks up resolved env-backed `apiKey` values and replaces them before persistence, with docs explicitly stating regenerated `models.json` stores env var names or SecretRef markers instead of resolved plaintext.
+  - file: [src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize.ts:77](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize.ts#L77)
+- **Config-read surfaces redact apiKey values:** `config.get` responds with `redactConfigSnapshot(...)`, and redaction tests explicitly cover `models.providers.openai.apiKey` so gateway config inspection no longer returns plaintext keys.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-methods/config.ts:397](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/gateway/server-methods/config.ts#L397)
+- **Status-path review found no current key-printing formatter:** A repo search across `src/commands/status*` found no API-key masking/printing path; the remaining auth label helper returns only source labels like `api-key (models.json)`, not secret fragments.
+  - file: [src/agents/model-auth-label.ts:60](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/model-auth-label.ts#L60)
+  - command: `rg -n "resolveModelAuthLabel|authLabel|maskApiKey|models.json\)|api-key \(" src/commands/status* src/commands -g '!**/*.test.ts'`
+- **Release notes document shipped models.json secret hardening:** The changelog for `2026.3.7` includes `Secrets/models.json persistence hardening` and `Models/provider apiKey persistence hardening`, matching the leak surface described in this issue.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:3006](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/CHANGELOG.md#L3006)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- This was a read-only code and history review; I did not run a live provider-capture reproduction in this checkout.
+- `gh` inspection was unavailable here because `GH_TOKEN` was not set, so the decision relies on repository code/docs/history already present locally.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
+
+Current `main` no longer carries resolved provider secrets through the model catalog or agent system prompt, and the remaining config-read surfaces redact provider `apiKey` values.
+
+What I checked:
+
+- **Model catalog shape is metadata-only:** `ModelCatalogEntry` contains only `id`, `name`, `provider`, `alias`, `contextWindow`, `reasoning`, and `input`, and `loadModelCatalog()` copies only those fields into the runtime catalog array. (`src/agents/model-catalog.types.ts:3`)
+- **Catalog loader strips provider secret fields:** The catalog loader iterates discovered entries and pushes only `{ id, name, provider, contextWindow, reasoning, input }`, so provider `apiKey`/header data is not retained in the prompt-visible catalog object. (`src/agents/model-catalog.ts:141`)
+- **System prompt gets aliases, not provider config:** Embedded runs pass `buildModelAliasLines(params.config)` into prompt construction; that helper emits only `- alias: provider/model`, and the system prompt renders only that alias section. (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:914`)
+- **models.json persistence now normalizes secrets to markers:** Provider normalization reverse-looks up resolved env-backed `apiKey` values and replaces them before persistence, with docs explicitly stating regenerated `models.json` stores env var names or SecretRef markers instead of resolved plaintext. (`src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize.ts:77`)
+- **Config-read surfaces redact apiKey values:** `config.get` responds with `redactConfigSnapshot(...)`, and redaction tests explicitly cover `models.providers.openai.apiKey` so gateway config inspection no longer returns plaintext keys. (`src/gateway/server-methods/config.ts:397`)
+- **Status-path review found no current key-printing formatter:** A repo search across `src/commands/status*` found no API-key masking/printing path; the remaining auth label helper returns only source labels like `api-key (models.json)`, not secret fragments. (`src/agents/model-auth-label.ts:60`)
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
+

+## Summary

The repo documents channel plugins as the supported extension path and has an explicit maintainer auto-close reason for third-party plugin requests.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Vision: optional capability ships as plugins:** Current project direction says OpenClaw has an extensive plugin API, core should stay lean, plugins should usually live outside core, and plugin authors should host and maintain them in their own repository.
+  - file: [VISION.md:54](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/VISION.md#L54)

+- **Plugin docs: channels are plugin capabilities:** The main plugin docs say plugins extend OpenClaw with channels and can be external community packages installed with `openclaw plugins install`.
+  - file: [docs/tools/plugin.md:11](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/tools/plugin.md#L11)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/tools/plugin.md | sed -n '1,120p'`

+- **Building guide: no need to add plugin to this repo:** The plugin authoring guide says you do not need to add a plugin to the OpenClaw repository and should publish it to ClawHub or npm instead.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:16](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/plugins/building-plugins.md#L16)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/building-plugins.md | sed -n '1,80p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Channel SDK guide exists for messaging integrations:** There is a dedicated guide for building a messaging channel plugin, which describes channels as plugin-owned integrations over shared core messaging primitives.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md:11](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md#L11)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md | sed -n '1,80p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Community plugin docs already treat channels as third-party plugins:** The community plugin page says third-party packages can extend OpenClaw with new channels and should be published on ClawHub or npm.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/community.md:9](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/plugins/community.md#L9)

+  - file: [.github/workflows/auto-response.yml:81](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/.github/workflows/auto-response.yml#L81)

+- **No XMTP or Convos implementation on current main:** A repo-wide text search for `XMTP|Convos|xmtp|convos` returned no matches, so this is not an already-landed feature on current main.
+  - command: `rg -n "XMTP|Convos|xmtp|convos" -S .`

+  - file: [docs/channels/slack.md:553](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/channels/slack.md#L553)

+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/slack/src/draft-stream.test.ts | sed -n '40,60p'`

+# [#11393: Route heartbeats to isolated system session (avoid thread pollution)](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11393)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11393](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11393)
+
+Author: RalphPepperoni
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 7, 2026, 19:25 UTC
+
+Updated at: Feb 10, 2026, 07:13 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already addresses the reported heartbeat-thread pollution: heartbeat OK replies are suppressed by default, heartbeats skip while the main or resolved session lane is busy, and an isolated `:heartbeat` session mode exists for dedicated heartbeat runs. The same behavior is already present in release `v2026.4.22`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Busy-session guard on heartbeat runs:** `runHeartbeatOnce` skips when either the main command lane or the resolved session lane has queued work, returning `requests-in-flight` instead of injecting a heartbeat turn into an active conversation.
+  - file: [src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts:759](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts#L759)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts | sed -n '730,790p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Isolated heartbeat session support:** When `heartbeat.isolatedSession` is enabled, heartbeat runs are moved onto a sibling `:heartbeat` session key instead of the main conversation session.
+  - file: [src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts:803](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts#L803)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts | sed -n '800,930p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **HEARTBEAT_OK is silent by default:** Default heartbeat visibility is `showOk: false`, and `maybeSendHeartbeatOk` only sends an OK message when `showOk` is explicitly enabled.
+  - file: [src/infra/heartbeat-visibility.ts:11](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/infra/heartbeat-visibility.ts#L11)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/heartbeat-visibility.ts | sed -n '1,40p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **HEARTBEAT_OK suppression in runner and tests:** The runner normalizes heartbeat replies and skips outbound delivery for OK-only acknowledgments; tests cover that Telegram gets no delivery when `showOk` is false.
+  - file: [src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts:1003](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts#L1003)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts | sed -n '995,1045p' && nl -ba src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts | sed -n '1090,1143p' && nl -ba src/infra/heartbeat-runner.respects-ackmaxchars-heartbeat-acks.test.ts | sed -n '217,228p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Docs and shipped release match current behavior:** Docs state that `HEARTBEAT_OK` is treated as an ack and dropped, and the diff from `v2026.4.22` to current `main` shows only a doc wording change plus a non-behavioral import-path change in `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts` for the relevant implementation.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/heartbeat.md:82](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/docs/gateway/heartbeat.md#L82)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/gateway/heartbeat.md | sed -n '80,91p' && git diff 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5..86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091 -- src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts docs/start/openclaw.md`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The issue title asks for isolated routing specifically; current behavior solves the reported pollution through default OK suppression and busy-session skipping, while full isolated-session routing remains opt-in via `heartbeat.isolatedSession`.
+
+## Close Comment

+- **Isolated heartbeat session support:** When `heartbeat.isolatedSession` is enabled, heartbeat runs are moved onto a sibling `:heartbeat` session key instead of the main conversation session. ([`src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts:803`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06a/src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts#L803), [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))
+- **HEARTBEAT_OK is silent by default:** Default heartbeat visibility is `showOk: false`, and `maybeSendHeartbeatOk` only sends an OK message when `showOk` is explicitly enabled. ([`src/infra/heartbeat-visibility.ts:11`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06a/src/infra/heartbeat-visibility.ts#L11), [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))
+- **HEARTBEAT_OK suppression in runner and tests:** The runner normalizes heartbeat replies and skips outbound delivery for OK-only acknowledgments; tests cover that Telegram gets no delivery when `showOk` is false. ([`src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts:1003`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06a/src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts#L1003), [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))
+- **Docs and shipped release match current behavior:** Docs state that `HEARTBEAT_OK` is treated as an ack and dropped, and the diff from `v2026.4.22` to current `main` shows only a doc wording change plus a non-behavioral import-path change in [`src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376/src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts) for the relevant implementation. Public docs: [`docs/gateway/heartbeat.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/heartbeat). ([`docs/gateway/heartbeat.md:82`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/heartbeat), [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376))
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.

+# [#11394: [Feature]: MCP Server tool-level allow/deny filtering](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11394)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11394](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11394)

+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already supports MCP tool-level filtering through the normal tool policy system: bundle/configured MCP tools are exposed as individual tool IDs in `serverName__toolName` form, and the existing `tools.allow` / `tools.deny` pipeline supports wildcard matching on those names. That delivers the requested allow/deny behavior, even though it uses the general tool-policy surface rather than a nested `mcp.servers.<name>.tools` block.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Docs say bundle MCP tools can be filtered by individual exposed tool names:** `docs/plugins/bundles.md` documents that bundle MCP tools are registered as `serverName__toolName`, and that profile allowlists/deny lists can include either individual exposed tool names or the `bundle-mcp` plugin key.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/bundles.md:162](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/plugins/bundles.md#L162)
+  - command: `sed -n '96,182p' docs/plugins/bundles.md`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Tool policy supports wildcard allow/deny patterns:** `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` documents `tools.allow` / `tools.deny` as case-insensitive and supporting `*` wildcards, which covers patterns like `my-server__read_*` and `my-server__delete_*`.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md:2234](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md#L2234)
+  - command: `sed -n '2200,2268p' docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Implementation registers each MCP tool as an individual tool name:** `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-materialize.ts` builds a distinct safe tool name for every MCP tool using the server prefix, then attaches plugin metadata so the normal tool-policy pipeline can filter it.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-materialize.ts:90](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-materialize.ts#L90)
+  - command: `sed -n '84,118p' src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-materialize.ts`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Wildcard matching is implemented in the shared tool-policy matcher:** `src/agents/tool-policy-match.ts` compiles glob patterns for both allow and deny lists and evaluates tool names against them.
+  - file: [src/agents/tool-policy-match.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/tool-policy-match.ts#L1)
+  - command: `sed -n '1,120p' src/agents/tool-policy-match.ts`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Shipped release already contains the docs proving individual MCP tool filtering:** The latest release commit includes the same `docs/plugins/bundles.md` text stating that allowlists and deny lists can include individual exposed MCP tool names or the `bundle-mcp` plugin key.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/bundles.md:174](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/docs/plugins/bundles.md#L174)
+  - command: `git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:docs/plugins/bundles.md | sed -n '174,179p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The implementation uses the general `tools.allow` / `tools.deny` surface on generated `serverName__toolName` IDs, not the exact nested `mcp.servers.<name>.tools.allow/deny` config shape proposed in the issue.
+

That delivers the requested allow/deny behavior, even though it uses the general tool-policy surface rather than a nested `mcp.servers.<name>.tools` block.

+# [#11504: [Feature Request] Add before_model_select plugin hook for dynamic model routing](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11504)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11504](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11504)

+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already ships the requested pre-model-selection plugin seam under the explicit `before_model_resolve` hook, with `before_prompt_build` as the companion post-session hook. The runtime invokes `before_model_resolve` before `resolveModelAsync()`, the SDK/types/docs expose it as the preferred path for model/provider override work, and the behavior is covered by wiring and priority-merge tests. This was already present in release `v2026.4.22`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Hook API exists for model/provider override:** `before_model_resolve` is defined as a typed plugin hook with `prompt` and optional attachment metadata input plus `modelOverride` / `providerOverride` output.
+  - file: [src/plugins/hook-before-agent-start.types.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/plugins/hook-before-agent-start.types.ts#L1)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/hook-before-agent-start.types.ts | sed -n '1,120p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Runtime runs hook before model resolution:** `resolveHookModelSelection()` runs `before_model_resolve` early so plugins can override provider/model before `resolveModel()`, and `run.ts` applies that selection before calling `resolveModelAsync()`.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/setup.ts:44](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/setup.ts#L44)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/setup.ts | sed -n '1,180p'`

+# [#11521: Feature: Automatic Markdown to Slack mrkdwn conversion](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11521)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11521](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11521)
+

+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already implements automatic Markdown-to-Slack-mrkdwn conversion for Slack outbound text, including normal send and slash-reply delivery paths, with regression coverage and a changelog entry showing it shipped in the `2026.1.13` release.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Slack formatter converts Markdown to mrkdwn:** `markdownToSlackMrkdwn` maps shared Markdown IR to Slack markers (`*`, `_`, `~`, code fences, `<url|label>` links) and `normalizeSlackOutboundText` reuses that conversion.
+  - file: [extensions/slack/src/format.ts:121](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/extensions/slack/src/format.ts#L121)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/slack/src/format.ts | sed -n '100,170p'`
+- **Outbound Slack sends auto-convert message text:** `sendMessageSlack` resolves Slack chunking/table settings, then runs each outbound Markdown chunk through `markdownToSlackMrkdwnChunks(...)` before posting to Slack.
+  - file: [extensions/slack/src/send.ts:359](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/extensions/slack/src/send.ts#L359)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/slack/src/send.ts | sed -n '356,382p'`
+- **Slash replies also auto-convert:** `deliverSlackSlashReplies` applies the same Markdown-to-mrkdwn conversion to reply chunks before responding to Slack slash commands.
+  - file: [extensions/slack/src/monitor/replies.ts:193](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/extensions/slack/src/monitor/replies.ts#L193)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/slack/src/monitor/replies.ts | sed -n '185,216p'`
+- **Regression tests cover expected conversions:** Tests assert bold, strike, links, lists, headings, blockquotes, escaping, and chunking behavior for Slack mrkdwn conversion.
+  - file: [extensions/slack/src/format.test.ts:9](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/extensions/slack/src/format.test.ts#L9)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/slack/src/format.test.ts | sed -n '1,95p'`
+- **Changelog says this shipped already:** The changelog entry under release `2026.1.13` says: `Messaging: unify markdown formatting + format-first chunking for Slack/Telegram/Signal. (#920)`
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:6386](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/CHANGELOG.md#L6386)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '6368,6394p'`
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The exact fixing commit SHA could not be determined from the shallow checkout, so `fixedSha` is left null.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
+
+Current `main` already implements automatic Markdown-to-Slack-mrkdwn conversion for Slack outbound text, including normal send and slash-reply delivery paths, with regression coverage and a changelog entry showing it shipped in the `2026.1.13` release.
+
+What I checked:
+
+- **Slack formatter converts Markdown to mrkdwn:** `markdownToSlackMrkdwn` maps shared Markdown IR to Slack markers (`*`, `_`, `~`, code fences, `<url|label>` links) and `normalizeSlackOutboundText` reuses that conversion. (`extensions/slack/src/format.ts:121`)
+- **Outbound Slack sends auto-convert message text:** `sendMessageSlack` resolves Slack chunking/table settings, then runs each outbound Markdown chunk through `markdownToSlackMrkdwnChunks(...)` before posting to Slack. (`extensions/slack/src/send.ts:359`)
+- **Slash replies also auto-convert:** `deliverSlackSlashReplies` applies the same Markdown-to-mrkdwn conversion to reply chunks before responding to Slack slash commands. (`extensions/slack/src/monitor/replies.ts:193`)
+- **Regression tests cover expected conversions:** Tests assert bold, strike, links, lists, headings, blockquotes, escaping, and chunking behavior for Slack mrkdwn conversion. (`extensions/slack/src/format.test.ts:9`)
+- **Changelog says this shipped already:** The changelog entry under release `2026.1.13` says: `Messaging: unify markdown formatting + format-first chunking for Slack/Telegram/Signal. (#920)`. (`CHANGELOG.md:6386`)
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
+

+# [#11533: [Feature]: Support DeepInfra as an LLM provider in OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11533)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11533](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11533)

+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already covers the DeepInfra report through the generic OpenAI-compatible provider path: non-OpenAI `/v1` endpoints are treated as proxy-style `openai-completions`, `reasoning_content` is parsed, and slash-bearing model IDs now resolve correctly in selection and allowlist flows.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Custom OpenAI-compatible provider path is documented:** The provider docs describe configuring custom `models.providers.<id>` entries with `api: "openai-completions"` and explicitly call out non-native OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/model-providers.md:603](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/concepts/model-providers.md#L603)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/concepts/model-providers.md | sed -n '600,620p'`
+- **Non-native endpoints disable unsupported developer-role behavior:** OpenClaw forces `supportsDeveloperRole: false` for non-default/non-public OpenAI-compatible endpoints, matching the kind of 4xx compatibility failure described in the issue.
+  - file: [src/agents/openai-completions-compat.ts:98](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/openai-completions-compat.ts#L98)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/openai-completions-compat.ts | sed -n '50,105p'`
+- **`reasoning_content` is consumed by the OpenAI-compatible transport:** The streaming transport explicitly reads `reasoning_content`, `reasoning`, and `reasoning_text`, which covers the DeepInfra response shape shown in the issue body.
+  - file: [src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:1436](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts#L1436)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts | sed -n '1430,1450p'`
+- **Slash-bearing model IDs are inferred from configured providers:** Model selection now infers the provider when the configured model ID itself contains slashes, fixing the comment's `vendor/modelname` allowlist resolution problem.
+  - file: [src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts:52](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts#L52)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts | sed -n '45,110p'`
+- **Allowlist resolution uses the inferred provider for bare model switches:** When a user switches models with a bare model ID, allowlist resolution now reuses the inferred provider instead of defaulting incorrectly.
+  - file: [src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts:610](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts#L610)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts | sed -n '603,632p'`
+- **Regression tests cover multi-segment provider/model IDs:** Current tests assert that multi-segment model IDs are preserved and resolved correctly through the runtime selection path.
+  - file: [src/agents/simple-completion-runtime.selection.test.ts:5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/simple-completion-runtime.selection.test.ts#L5)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/simple-completion-runtime.selection.test.ts | sed -n '1,70p'`
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- No live DeepInfra request was run in this read-only review; the conclusion is based on current code, docs, and tests.
+- There is still no bundled DeepInfra-specific provider plugin or provider doc page, so this close assumes the generic OpenAI-compatible path is sufficient for the original request.
+

([`docs/concepts/model-providers.md:603`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/model-providers))

+- **`reasoning_content` is consumed by the OpenAI-compatible transport:** The streaming transport explicitly reads `reasoning_content`, `reasoning`, and `reasoning_text`, which covers the DeepInfra response shape shown in the issue body. (`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:1436`)

+- **Gateway/subsystem logs call the shared local-time formatter:** Subsystem console rendering routes both JSON and pretty/timestamp-prefix output through `formatConsoleTimestamp(...)`.

+- **Timezone resolution falls back to system timezone:** When no explicit timezone is passed, logging timestamp formatting uses `process.env.TZ` if valid, otherwise `Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone`, which matches the feature request to use local/system time.

+# [#11619: Periodic memory auto-save](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11619)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11619](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11619)
+
+Author: murilo
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 8, 2026, 02:33 UTC
+
+Updated at: Feb 23, 2026, 04:24 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: belongs on ClawHub
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+The request is a valid idea, but current main’s documented scope and extension seams place periodic memory persistence in plugin/community-plugin territory rather than OpenClaw core.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Core scope prefers plugins for optional capability:** VISION says core should stay lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and memory is an exclusive plugin slot.
+  - file: [VISION.md:54](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/VISION.md#L54)
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Bundled session-memory is intentionally narrow:** The bundled hook is documented as saving session context only when `/new` or `/reset` is issued.
+  - file: [src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/HOOK.md:19](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/HOOK.md#L19)
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Current implementation only fires on reset/new:** The handler exits unless the event is a `command` with action `new` or `reset`, with no periodic/token autosave path in core.
+  - file: [src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.ts:53](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.ts#L53)
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Plugin SDK already exposes the needed extension seams:** OpenClaw plugins can register event hooks and background services, and memory plugins can register memory capability and pre-compaction flush behavior.
+  - file: [src/plugins/types.ts:1928](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/plugins/types.ts#L1928)
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **No built-in periodic autosave feature exists on main:** A repo search found no `memory-autosave`, `autosave`, `triggerTokens`, or `triggerMinutes` implementation under the relevant core docs/code paths.
+  - command: `rg -n "triggerTokens|triggerMinutes|memory-autosave|autosave" src/hooks docs/automation docs/cli docs/plugins src/plugins src/agents -g '!**/dist/**' -g '!extensions/diffs/assets/**'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Some users may still want a bundled default autosave policy; closing this as scope does not solve that product demand.
+- The issue body proposes hook config in core terms, so closing should clearly point future work toward a plugin/community-plugin implementation path.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing this as better suited for [ClawHub](https://clawhub.ai/)/community plugin work after Codex review.
+
+The request is a valid idea, but current main’s documented scope and extension seams place periodic memory persistence in plugin/community-plugin territory rather than OpenClaw core.
+
+What I checked:
+
+- **Core scope prefers plugins for optional capability:** [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says core should stay lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and memory is an exclusive plugin slot. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06a/VISION.md#L54), [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))
+- **Bundled session-memory is intentionally narrow:** The bundled hook is documented as saving session context only when `/new` or `/reset` is issued. ([`src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/HOOK.md:19`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06a/src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/HOOK.md#L19), [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))
+- **Current implementation only fires on reset/new:** The handler exits unless the event is a `command` with action `new` or `reset`, with no periodic/token autosave path in core. ([`src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.ts:53`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06a/src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.ts#L53), [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))
+- **Plugin SDK already exposes the needed extension seams:** OpenClaw plugins can register event hooks and background services, and memory plugins can register memory capability and pre-compaction flush behavior. ([`src/plugins/types.ts:1928`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06a/src/plugins/types.ts#L1928), [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))
+- **No built-in periodic autosave feature exists on main:** A repo search found no `memory-autosave`, `autosave`, `triggerTokens`, or `triggerMinutes` implementation under the relevant core docs/code paths. ([86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))

+Confidence: high

+## Summary
+
+Current main implements the observable request through the existing responsePrefix cascade with model-aware template variables. Slack replies are wired through the shared reply pipeline, the selected provider/model is captured before delivery, and outbound normalization prepends the resolved prefix to Slack reply text. This supports per-channel/account Slack prefixes such as model/provider labels or Slack custom emoji shortcodes without adding the exact modelPrefixes/prefixTemplate shape proposed in the issue.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Slack reply pipeline captures selected model context:** Slack dispatch creates the channel reply pipeline for channel "slack" and later passes onModelSelected into dispatchInboundMessage, so the actual selected model is available before reply normalization/delivery.
+  - file: [extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts:355](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts#L355)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts | sed -n '230,430p'; nl -ba extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts | sed -n '940,985p'`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Prefix context includes provider, short model, full model, and thinking level:** createReplyPrefixContext records provider, short model, modelFull, and thinkingLevel in the response prefix context, then resolves the effective messages config with channel/account specificity.
+  - file: [src/channels/reply-prefix.ts:35](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/src/channels/reply-prefix.ts#L35)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/channels/reply-prefix.ts | sed -n '1,260p'`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Outbound reply normalization prepends resolved template prefix:** normalizeReplyPayload resolves responsePrefix template variables from the model context and prepends the effective prefix to outbound reply text when present.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.ts:112](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.ts#L112)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.ts | sed -n '90,150p'`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Public docs document per-channel/account responsePrefix and model variables:** The config docs list channels.<channel>.responsePrefix and channels.<channel>.accounts.<id>.responsePrefix plus template variables {model}, {modelFull}, {provider}, {thinkingLevel}, and {identity.name}.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/config-agents.md:1220](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/docs/gateway/config-agents.md#L1220)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/gateway/config-agents.md | sed -n '1180,1235p'`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Regression coverage proves Slack delivery uses selected runtime model in prefix templates:** The delivery test normalizes a Slack reply with messages.responsePrefix="[{modelFull}]" and verifies the delivered text is prefixed with the selected runtime provider/model.
+  - file: [src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts:123](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts#L123)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts | sed -n '110,170p'`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Proof commit is on main after the latest release tag:** git merge-base --is-ancestor 607bc53ff38c9ab2e83495bf57247bc63541d0eb v2026.4.24 exited 1, while the same check against HEAD exited 0, so I did not find a shipped release tag for this main-only implementation.
+  - command: `git merge-base --is-ancestor 607bc53f v2026.4.24; git merge-base --is-ancestor 607bc53f HEAD; git tag --contains 607bc53ff38c9ab2e83495bf57247bc63541d0eb`
+  - sha: [607bc53ff38c](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/607bc53ff38c9ab2e83495bf57247bc63541d0eb)

+# [#11826: Support payload.model override for per-job model selection](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11826)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11826](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11826)
+
+Author: similar-bespoke
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 8, 2026, 11:33 UTC
+
+Updated at: Feb 10, 2026, 21:01 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already supports per-job cron `payload.model` overrides end-to-end. The cron payload and patch types include `model`, the Gateway protocol accepts it for `cron.update`, the cron tool exposes/recovers it, runtime model selection applies it with allowlist handling, and regression tests cover a model-only `cron.update` patch. The implementation was already present in release `v2026.4.22`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Cron payload types include `model` for jobs and patches:** `CronAgentTurnPayloadFields` defines optional `model?: string`, and `CronAgentTurnPayloadPatch` inherits it, so stored jobs and `cron.update` patches can carry a per-job model override.
+  - file: [src/cron/types.ts:116](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/cron/types.ts#L116)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/cron/types.ts | sed -n '112,142p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Gateway protocol schema accepts `payload.model`:** `cronAgentTurnPayloadSchema` includes `model`, and `CronPayloadPatchSchema` reuses that schema for update patches, so `cron.update` no longer rejects `payload.model` at validation time.
+  - file: [src/gateway/protocol/schema/cron.ts:4](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/gateway/protocol/schema/cron.ts#L4)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/protocol/schema/cron.ts | sed -n '1,170p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Gateway test proves model-only `cron.update` patch succeeds:** The server test sends `rpcReq(ws, "cron.update", { patch: { payload: { model: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6" }}})` and expects success plus the updated model in the returned payload.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server.cron.test.ts:409](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/gateway/server.cron.test.ts#L409)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server.cron.test.ts | sed -n '409,430p'`

+## Summary
+
+Issue #11919 proposes expanding the core skill format into a dependency/interface/composition system, but current project direction keeps skills intentionally simple and pushes optional ecosystem capability toward plugins and ClawHub rather than new core orchestration layers.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Vision routes optional capability out of core:** Project vision says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, not core.
+  - file: [VISION.md:55](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/VISION.md#L55)

+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Vision sends new skills to ClawHub first:** Core skill additions are explicitly rare, with ClawHub as the preferred path.
+  - file: [VISION.md:69](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/VISION.md#L69)
+  - command: `nl -ba VISION.md | sed -n '45,110p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
