+  - file: [VISION.md:107](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/VISION.md#L107)
+  - command: `nl -ba VISION.md | sed -n '45,110p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Current skills contract is intentionally flat and AgentSkills-compatible:** Docs describe skills as AgentSkills-compatible folders with load-time filtering, not dependency graphs or interface resolution.
+  - file: [docs/tools/skills.md:9](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/tools/skills.md#L9)

+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Plugin path already exists for shipping skills:** Docs already route extensibility through plugins that can ship skills and through ClawHub install/sync flows.
+  - file: [docs/tools/skills.md:82](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/tools/skills.md#L82)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/tools/skills.md | sed -n '1,220p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Core parser only supports narrow skill metadata:** The typed skill metadata includes only gating/install fields such as os, requires, primaryEnv, and install; no dependency/interface/composition fields exist in the core contract.
+  - file: [src/agents/skills/types.ts:19](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/skills/types.ts#L19)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/skills/types.ts | sed -n '1,120p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Prompt builder expects a flat list of skills:** The runtime formatter emits a simple <available_skills> list and tells the model to read a matching skill file on demand, which matches the documented flat-skill model rather than composed dependency resolution.
+  - file: [src/agents/skills/skill-contract.ts:44](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/skills/skill-contract.ts#L44)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/skills/skill-contract.ts | sed -n '35,75p'`

+## Summary
+
+Issue #11940 is best closed as a ClawHub/community plugin request, not kept open for OpenClaw core. Current project scope explicitly keeps optional capability out of core, prefers externally hosted plugins, and uses ClawHub as the canonical discovery surface for community plugins. The requested feature is already framed as an external UI plugin in its own repository, and current main already exposes model/fallback/auth surfaces that such a plugin can build on.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Vision: optional capability should stay plugin-side:** `VISION.md` says OpenClaw has an extensive plugin API, core stays lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high.
+  - file: [VISION.md:54](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/VISION.md#L54)

+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Vision: plugin authors should host and maintain their own repo:** `VISION.md` explicitly says the preferred path is npm distribution plus local extension loading for development, and if you build a plugin, host and maintain it in your own repository.
+  - file: [VISION.md:57](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/VISION.md#L57)
+  - command: `nl -ba VISION.md | sed -n '48,76p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Community plugin policy points discovery to ClawHub:** `docs/plugins/community.md` says community plugins are third-party packages published on ClawHub or npm, and that ClawHub is the canonical discovery surface for community plugins.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/community.md:9](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/plugins/community.md#L9)

+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Current main already exposes model-management surfaces:** The Control UI already supports session model overrides via `sessions.list`/`sessions.patch` and debug model snapshots via `models.list`; model priority/fallback configuration is documented in the CLI/model docs via `agents.defaults.model.primary`, `agents.defaults.model.fallbacks`, and `openclaw models fallbacks ...`. That means a specialized model-priority UI can remain an external plugin rather than a core requirement.
+  - file: [docs/web/control-ui.md:111](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/web/control-ui.md#L111)

+- **Vision: optional capability should stay plugin-side:** [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says OpenClaw has an extensive plugin API, core stays lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06a/VISION.md#L54), [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))
+- **Vision: plugin authors should host and maintain their own repo:** [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) explicitly says the preferred path is npm distribution plus local extension loading for development, and if you build a plugin, host and maintain it in your own repository. ([`VISION.md:57`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06a/VISION.md#L57), [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))
+- **Community plugin policy points discovery to ClawHub:** [`docs/plugins/community.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/community) says community plugins are third-party packages published on ClawHub or npm, and that ClawHub is the canonical discovery surface for community plugins. ([`docs/plugins/community.md:9`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/community), [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))
+- **Current main already exposes model-management surfaces:** The Control UI already supports session model overrides via `sessions.list`/`sessions.patch` and debug model snapshots via `models.list`; model priority/fallback configuration is documented in the CLI/model docs via `agents.defaults.model.primary`, `agents.defaults.model.fallbacks`, and `openclaw models fallbacks ...`. That means a specialized model-priority UI can remain an external plugin rather than a core requirement. Public docs: [`docs/web/control-ui.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/web/control-ui). ([`docs/web/control-ui.md:111`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/web/control-ui), [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06a))
+
+So I’m closing this as a scope-fit item for the plugin/community path rather than keeping it open as an OpenClaw core request.
+
+Review notes: reviewed against [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091).

+
+Type: issue

+## Summary
+
+Current main already has post-conversation/session hook primitives and shipped examples that cover this request well enough: internal hooks, plugin `agent_end`/`session_end` lifecycle hooks, a bundled `session-memory` hook that writes transcript summaries to `memory/`, and a memory plugin that auto-captures from `agent_end`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Internal hook system and bundled session-memory hook are documented:** OpenClaw documents internal Gateway hooks, hook packs, and a bundled `session-memory` hook that saves session context to `<workspace>/memory/YYYY-MM-DD-slug.md`.
+  - file: [docs/automation/hooks.md:152](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096/docs/automation/hooks.md#L152)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/automation/hooks.md | sed -n '1,220p'`
+  - sha: [37c250695b60](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096)
+- **Plugin lifecycle includes session_end with end reasons and transcript path:** The plugin hook contract includes `session_end` reasons (`new`, `reset`, `idle`, `daily`, `compaction`, `deleted`, `unknown`) and exposes `sessionFile`, `transcriptArchived`, and next-session identifiers.
+  - file: [src/plugins/hook-types.ts:390](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096/src/plugins/hook-types.ts#L390)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/hook-types.ts | sed -n '390,410p'`
+  - sha: [37c250695b60](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096)
+- **Gateway emits session_end hooks with archived transcript context:** When a session ends, the gateway resolves a stable transcript path and emits `session_end` with the reason, transcript file, archive status, and replacement session ids when present.
+  - file: [src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts:107](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096/src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts#L107)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts | sed -n '107,135p'`
+  - sha: [37c250695b60](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096)
+- **Bundled session-memory hook reads prior transcript content and writes memory files:** The shipped `session-memory` handler runs on `/new` and `/reset`, recovers the prior session transcript, extracts recent conversation content, generates a slug, and writes a Markdown file under the workspace `memory/` directory.
+  - file: [src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.ts:53](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096/src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.ts#L53)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.ts | sed -n '45,235p'`
+  - sha: [37c250695b60](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/37c250695b600fb12afc799ee1b32d7aae73d096)
+- **Agent-end auto-capture already exists in a memory plugin:** The bundled `memory-lancedb` plugin registers `agent_end` and uses it to analyze user messages and store capturable memories automatically after a turn completes.
+  - file: [extensions/memory-lancedb/index.ts:612](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/extensions/memory-lancedb/index.ts#L612)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/memory-lancedb/index.ts | sed -n '600,675p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Tests cover session_end wiring and emitted reasons:** Tests verify that `session_end` is invoked and carries archived transcript metadata for reset/delete flows.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server.sessions.gateway-server-sessions-a.test.ts:2513](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/gateway/server.sessions.gateway-server-sessions-a.test.ts#L2513)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server.sessions.gateway-server-sessions-a.test.ts | sed -n '2510,2558p' && nl -ba src/gateway/server.sessions.gateway-server-sessions-a.test.ts | sed -n '3075,3140p'`
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Current main provides the hook mechanism and example implementations, not one single built-in structured knowledge-extraction product surface. More opinionated routing or UX could still be added later without changing the fact that the core mechanism now exists.
+- The checkout history is shallow, so I could identify the introducing implementation commit by blame, but I could not prove a released tag that contains it.

Current main has reactive rate-limit classification, auth-profile cooldowns, and fallback, but not the requested proactive `requestsPerMinute`/`minDelayMs` provider throttle, so this should close as duplicate/superseded rather than implemented.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Protected-close guard:** GitHub API shows #12135 is authored by arl1984 with author_association NONE and only the enhancement label; no protected labels are present.

+- **Canonical open tracker:** Open issue #13615, “Add rate limiting and throttling for external API calls,” explicitly proposes per-provider limits, queuing, automatic 429 backoff, and usage visibility for LLM providers and other external APIs.
+  - command: `curl -sS -L https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/issues/13615`
+- **Current provider config has no rateLimit field:** `ModelProviderConfig` includes base URL, auth, headers, request overrides, and models, but no `rateLimit`, `requestsPerMinute`, or `minDelayMs` throttle setting.
+  - file: [src/config/types.models.ts:115](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/src/config/types.models.ts#L115)

+- **Schema is strict and excludes requested config:** `ModelProviderSchema` is strict and only admits the existing provider fields, so the sample `models.providers.nvidia.rateLimit` config from #12135 is not accepted on current main.
+  - file: [src/config/zod-schema.core.ts:347](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/src/config/zod-schema.core.ts#L347)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/zod-schema.core.ts | sed -n '347,361p'`
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Current behavior is reactive failover/cooldown:** Current main classifies 429s as `rate_limit`, marks the failed profile, and may rotate profiles or fall back; this mitigates failures after they occur but does not proactively pace calls for a free-tier provider.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts:1481](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts#L1481)

+## Summary

+The issue asks for a public HTTP/HTTPS multi-user chatroom/account system. Current OpenClaw core intentionally keeps the built-in web UI as an operator/admin surface, while the plugin API already supports channel integrations and plugin-owned HTTP routes. A lighter public-web chatroom belongs as a ClawHub/plugin project; the existing Mattermost plugin remains the relevant bundled self-hosted chat option mentioned by the reporter.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Scope anchor:** VISION says OpenClaw core stays lean, optional capabilities should usually ship as plugins, and plugin discovery/promotion belongs in ClawHub.

+- **Built-in web UI is not a public chatroom:** Dashboard docs identify Control UI as an admin surface for chat/config/exec approvals and say not to expose it publicly; remote access is framed around localhost, Tailscale, SSH, shared-secret, or trusted-proxy operator auth.
+  - file: [docs/web/dashboard.md:33](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cbe5515b70abaeba98550b58d739ca0896907c43/docs/web/dashboard.md#L33)

+  - file: [docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:145](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cbe5515b70abaeba98550b58d739ca0896907c43/docs/plugins/building-plugins.md#L145)

+## Evidence

+- **Cleanup command on main:** `openclaw sessions cleanup` is registered with `--dry-run`, `--enforce`, `--fix-missing`, `--active-key`, `--agent`, `--all-agents`, `--store`, and `--json`, covering the bulk cleanup workflow requested here.

+- **Cleanup implementation:** `sessionsCleanupCommand` previews stale/missing/capped/budget cleanup and applies maintenance through `updateSessionStore` when not in dry-run mode.
+  - file: [src/commands/sessions-cleanup.ts:298](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/src/commands/sessions-cleanup.ts#L298)
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Agent-facing subagent kill:** The `subagents` agent tool is documented in code as listing, killing, or steering spawned sub-agents for the requester session; `action: "kill"` supports one target or all targets.
+  - file: [src/agents/tools/subagents-tool.ts:33](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/src/agents/tools/subagents-tool.ts#L33)
+  - sha: [1dfa52d0711d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f)
+- **Ownership and cascade semantics:** Subagent control enforces requester ownership and child-only scope, and kill paths cascade to descendant subagents, matching the original security constraint that agents should only kill their own sessions.
+  - file: [src/agents/subagent-control.ts:161](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/src/agents/subagent-control.ts#L161)

+  - file: [src/cron/session-reaper.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1dfa52d0711dfc86e1c04538fe46e3d43961766f/src/cron/session-reaper.ts#L1)

+## Summary

+Current `main` already provides programmatic cumulative session usage and cost access through Gateway RPCs and CLI JSON surfaces, including per-session totals, per-model/provider aggregates, and per-session cumulative timeseries.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Gateway RPC surface:** `usage.cost`, `sessions.usage`, `sessions.usage.timeseries`, and `sessions.usage.logs` are implemented as Gateway handlers.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server-methods/usage.ts:397](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/gateway/server-methods/usage.ts#L397)
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Per-session cumulative summaries:** `loadSessionCostSummary` accumulates input/output/cache/total tokens, total cost, daily breakdowns, message counts, tool usage, and per-model usage from transcript usage entries.
+  - file: [src/infra/session-cost-usage.ts:554](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/infra/session-cost-usage.ts#L554)
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Per-session cumulative timeseries:** `loadSessionUsageTimeSeries` returns per-point token/cost data plus `cumulativeTokens` and `cumulativeCost` for a session.
+  - file: [src/infra/session-cost-usage.ts:826](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/infra/session-cost-usage.ts#L826)
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Typed programmatic aggregates:** `SessionsUsageResult` includes session rows plus aggregate rollups like `byModel`, `byProvider`, `byAgent`, `byChannel`, and daily stats.
+  - file: [src/shared/usage-types.ts:39](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/shared/usage-types.ts#L39)
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **CLI access:** The CLI exposes generic JSON Gateway calls and a dedicated `gateway usage-cost` command for programmatic retrieval.
+  - file: [src/cli/gateway-cli/register.ts:420](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/cli/gateway-cli/register.ts#L420)
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Public docs:** The Gateway protocol docs list `usage.cost`, `sessions.usage`, `sessions.usage.timeseries`, and `sessions.usage.logs` as public method families.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/protocol.md:281](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/gateway/protocol.md#L281)
+  - sha: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+- **Shipped release proof:** The `v2026.4.22` tag already contains the same RPC names in `src/gateway/server-methods/usage.ts`, so this was shipped before or with that release.
+  - command: `git show v2026.4.22:src/gateway/server-methods/usage.ts | rg -n 'usage\.cost|sessions\.usage|sessions\.usage\.timeseries|sessions\.usage\.logs'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions

+## Summary
+
+#12333 requests first-class project/context switching. Current main still exposes agents, sessions, and workspace configuration rather than a native project switcher, and open issue #13676 now tracks the same remaining first-class Projects work more completely, including project/session/workspace binding and Switch Project behavior.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Canonical open tracker:** Provided GitHub context shows #13676 is open and explicitly proposes a top-level Project object with Project-to-session binding, Project-to-workspace binding, project-scoped tools/skills, runtime constraints, and a Switch Project action. That covers the remaining CLI/UI/context-switching request from #12333.
+- **Current CLI command surface has no project/workspace switch command:** The core command catalog lists setup/onboard/config/agent/agents/sessions/tasks and related commands, but no `workspace` or `project` command. The existing relevant command is `agents`, described as managing isolated agents.

+# [#12405: Feature: Pluggable sandbox backends & per-agent exec routing](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12405)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12405](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12405)
+
+Author: anshumanbh
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 9, 2026, 06:59 UTC
+
+Updated at: Mar 16, 2026, 08:47 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [86f69ba5a06a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.3.31](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.3.31)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already implements both parts of this request: sandbox backends are pluggable (`docker`, `ssh`, and plugin-registered backends such as `openshell`), and exec defaults/routing can be overridden per agent via `agents.list[].tools.exec.*`. The feature is documented, covered by tests, and shipped by `v2026.3.31`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Pluggable sandbox backend registry exists in core:** Core keeps a backend registry and registers built-in `docker` and `ssh` backends; unknown backend ids fail with a message telling users to load the providing plugin or use Docker.
+  - file: [src/agents/sandbox/backend.ts:26](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/sandbox/backend.ts#L26)
+- **Plugin SDK exposes sandbox backend registration:** The public plugin SDK exports `registerSandboxBackend`, `getSandboxBackendFactory`, and related sandbox backend types, so sandbox runtimes are not Docker-only anymore.
+  - file: [src/plugin-sdk/sandbox.ts:24](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/plugin-sdk/sandbox.ts#L24)
+- **Bundled OpenShell plugin registers its own backend:** The OpenShell plugin calls `registerSandboxBackend("openshell", ...)`, proving non-core backends can be plugged into the sandbox layer.
+  - file: [extensions/openshell/index.ts:14](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/extensions/openshell/index.ts#L14)
+- **Docs and config describe multiple sandbox backends and per-agent sandbox overrides:** Docs list `docker`, `ssh`, and `openshell` under `agents.defaults.sandbox.backend`, and the agent config type allows per-agent `sandbox.backend` overrides.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/sandboxing.md:57](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/docs/gateway/sandboxing.md#L57)
+- **Per-agent exec routing/defaults are resolved over global defaults:** Exec defaults resolve in order `session -> agent -> global`, including `host`, `security`, `ask`, and `node`, so agents no longer share one global exec target.
+  - file: [src/agents/exec-defaults.ts:33](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/exec-defaults.ts#L33)
+- **Per-agent exec behavior is covered by tests:** Tests explicitly verify that agent-specific `tools.exec` defaults override global defaults and affect exec host behavior.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-tools-agent-config.exec.test.ts:113](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/src/agents/pi-tools-agent-config.exec.test.ts#L113)
+- **Release notes show both pieces shipped:** `v2026.3.22` added pluggable sandbox backends plus SSH/OpenShell support, and `v2026.3.31` added per-agent `tools.exec` defaults.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:2164](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/86f69ba5a06ae256906c3a26308801e0b84e0091/CHANGELOG.md#L2164)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- A later comment on the issue asks for broader per-agent filesystem/process isolation via gateway+isolated nodes. That is not the same as the original request and is not established by the evidence above.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
+
+Current `main` already implements both parts of this request: sandbox backends are pluggable (`docker`, `ssh`, and plugin-registered backends such as `openshell`), and exec defaults/routing can be overridden per agent via `agents.list[].tools.exec.*`. The feature is documented, covered by tests, and shipped by `v2026.3.31`.
+
+What I checked:
+
+- **Pluggable sandbox backend registry exists in core:** Core keeps a backend registry and registers built-in `docker` and `ssh` backends; unknown backend ids fail with a message telling users to load the providing plugin or use Docker. (`src/agents/sandbox/backend.ts:26`)
+- **Plugin SDK exposes sandbox backend registration:** The public plugin SDK exports `registerSandboxBackend`, `getSandboxBackendFactory`, and related sandbox backend types, so sandbox runtimes are not Docker-only anymore. (`src/plugin-sdk/sandbox.ts:24`)
+- **Bundled OpenShell plugin registers its own backend:** The OpenShell plugin calls `registerSandboxBackend("openshell", ...)`, proving non-core backends can be plugged into the sandbox layer. (`extensions/openshell/index.ts:14`)
+- **Docs and config describe multiple sandbox backends and per-agent sandbox overrides:** Docs list `docker`, `ssh`, and `openshell` under `agents.defaults.sandbox.backend`, and the agent config type allows per-agent `sandbox.backend` overrides. ([`docs/gateway/sandboxing.md:57`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/sandboxing))
+- **Per-agent exec routing/defaults are resolved over global defaults:** Exec defaults resolve in order `session -> agent -> global`, including `host`, `security`, `ask`, and `node`, so agents no longer share one global exec target. (`src/agents/exec-defaults.ts:33`)
+- **Per-agent exec behavior is covered by tests:** Tests explicitly verify that agent-specific `tools.exec` defaults override global defaults and affect exec host behavior. (`src/agents/pi-tools-agent-config.exec.test.ts:113`)
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
+

+# [#12509: [Feature Request] Plugin Command Registration Conflict Detection](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12509)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12509](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12509)
+
+Author: fr33d3m0n
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 9, 2026, 10:00 UTC
+
+Updated at: Feb 10, 2026, 07:01 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already blocks plugin command registration conflicts instead of silently letting later plugins override earlier ones, and that behavior is present in the latest release commit `00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5` (`v2026.4.22`).
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Duplicate plugin commands are rejected at registration time:** `registerPluginCommand(...)` enumerates the command name plus native aliases and returns `ok: false` with `Command "..." already registered by plugin "..."` if any invocation key is already owned, rather than overwriting the existing entry.
+  - file: [src/plugins/command-registration.ts:150](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/plugins/command-registration.ts#L150)
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Built-in command hijacking is also blocked:** `validateCommandName(...)` reserves core command names like `help`, `commands`, `debug`, `exec`, and `usage`, so plugins cannot register over security-sensitive built-ins.
+  - file: [src/plugins/command-registration.ts:32](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/plugins/command-registration.ts#L32)
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Plugin load path surfaces the conflict as an error:** The plugin registry calls `registerPluginCommand(...)` during activation and emits `command registration failed: ...` diagnostics when registration is rejected, so conflicts do not silently win by load order.
+  - file: [src/plugins/registry.ts:1151](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/plugins/registry.ts#L1151)
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Regression tests cover alias collisions and reserved-name rejection:** Tests assert that a second plugin trying to claim an existing alias gets `Command "pair_device" already registered by plugin "demo-plugin"`, and that aliases like `help` are rejected as reserved.
+  - file: [src/plugins/commands.test.ts:415](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/plugins/commands.test.ts#L415)
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Same conflict-detection code is shipped in the latest release:** `git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:src/plugins/command-registration.ts` shows the same duplicate-registration guard and error path in release `v2026.4.22`.
+  - file: [src/plugins/command-registration.ts:150](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/plugins/command-registration.ts#L150)
+  - command: `git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:src/plugins/command-registration.ts | nl -ba | sed -n '140,195p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The issue body also proposes manifest declarations, namespace isolation, and approval flows; those broader enhancements are not evidenced here. The close is justified only because the concrete conflict-detection/hijack claim is already addressed on `main` and in `v2026.4.22`.
+

+Current `main` already blocks plugin command registration conflicts instead of silently letting later plugins override earlier ones, and that behavior is present in the latest release commit `00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5` (`v2026.4.22`).

([`src/plugins/command-registration.ts:150`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e2/src/plugins/command-registration.ts#L150), [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e2))
+- **Built-in command hijacking is also blocked:** `validateCommandName(...)` reserves core command names like `help`, `commands`, `debug`, `exec`, and `usage`, so plugins cannot register over security-sensitive built-ins. ([`src/plugins/command-registration.ts:32`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e2/src/plugins/command-registration.ts#L32), [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e2))

([`src/plugins/registry.ts:1151`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e2/src/plugins/registry.ts#L1151), [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e2))
+- **Regression tests cover alias collisions and reserved-name rejection:** Tests assert that a second plugin trying to claim an existing alias gets `Command "pair_device" already registered by plugin "demo-plugin"`, and that aliases like `help` are rejected as reserved. ([`src/plugins/commands.test.ts:415`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e2/src/plugins/commands.test.ts#L415), [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e2))
+- **Same conflict-detection code is shipped in the latest release:** [`git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:src/plugins/command-registration.ts`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376/git%20show%2000bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5%3Asrc/plugins/command-registration.ts) shows the same duplicate-registration guard and error path in release `v2026.4.22`. +  - command: `rg --files extensions/zalouser/src | sed -n '1,120p'`

+  - file: [extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts:76](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts#L76)

+  - file: [extensions/nostr/src/types.ts:17](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/extensions/nostr/src/types.ts#L17)

+  - file: [docs/plugins/architecture.md:447](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/docs/plugins/architecture.md#L447)

+- **Zalo storage claim is broad but not enough for current-main security verification:** Current Zalo code still writes session credentials as JSON under the trusted local state directory, but the issue does not provide a current-main reproduction tying that storage choice to a documented OpenClaw trust-boundary bypass or OpenClaw-owned secret exposure.

+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)

+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12534](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12534)

+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.2.12](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.2.12)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main already protects the Nostr profile mutation surface behind gateway auth, trusted-operator runtime scope, explicit `operator.admin` checks, and loopback-only mutation guards. The changelog records this exact security issue as fixed in release `v2026.2.12`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Nostr route is not unauthenticated:** The Nostr profile HTTP route is registered with `auth: "gateway"` and `gatewayRuntimeScopeSurface: "trusted-operator"`, so it is not exposed as a plugin-managed public endpoint.
+  - file: [extensions/nostr/index.ts:86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/extensions/nostr/index.ts#L86)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/nostr/index.ts | sed -n '1,140p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Mutation handlers require admin scope and loopback-only access:** Before any profile update/import path can reach the config write path, the handler rejects callers missing `operator.admin`, rejects non-loopback remote addresses, rejects forwarded non-loopback client IPs, and rejects cross-site Origin/Referer requests.
+  - file: [extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile-http.ts:267](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile-http.ts#L267)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile-http.ts | sed -n '260,420p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Gateway blocks protected plugin routes without auth:** Gateway dispatch fails closed when a matched plugin route requires gateway auth and the request is unauthenticated, before invoking the plugin handler at all.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server/plugins-http.ts:85](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/gateway/server/plugins-http.ts#L85)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server/plugins-http.ts | sed -n '70,125p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Regression tests cover unauthenticated and remote mutation attempts:** Nostr tests explicitly reject non-loopback mutation requests and reject callers missing `operator.admin` for both PUT `/profile` and POST `/profile/import`.
+  - file: [extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile-http.test.ts:351](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile-http.test.ts#L351)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile-http.test.ts | sed -n '340,590p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Gateway auth coverage includes the Nostr profile path:** Gateway auth tests verify `/api/channels/nostr/default/profile` returns unauthorized without bearer auth and only passes through when authenticated.
+  - file: [src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts:230](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts#L230)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts | sed -n '230,285p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Release notes record this exact fix:** The `v2026.2.12` changelog includes: `Security: fix unauthenticated Nostr profile API remote config tampering. (#13719)`.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:5039](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/CHANGELOG.md#L5039)
+  - command: `git show v2026.4.22:CHANGELOG.md | nl -ba | sed -n '5018,5044p'`
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The checkout is shallow/grafted, so the exact fixing commit SHA could not be proven from local history.
+- `gh` inspection was unavailable because `GH_TOKEN` was not set, so the review relied on current code, tests, and release notes only.

+## Close Comment
+
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.

+Current main already protects the Nostr profile mutation surface behind gateway auth, trusted-operator runtime scope, explicit `operator.admin` checks, and loopback-only mutation guards. The changelog records this exact security issue as fixed in release `v2026.2.12`.
+
+What I checked:
+

([`extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile-http.ts:267`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e2/extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile-http.ts#L267), [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e2))
+- **Gateway blocks protected plugin routes without auth:** Gateway dispatch fails closed when a matched plugin route requires gateway auth and the request is unauthenticated, before invoking the plugin handler at all. ([`src/gateway/server/plugins-http.ts:85`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e2/src/gateway/server/plugins-http.ts#L85), [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e2))
+- **Regression tests cover unauthenticated and remote mutation attempts:** Nostr tests explicitly reject non-loopback mutation requests and reject callers missing `operator.admin` for both PUT `/profile` and POST `/profile/import`. ([`extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile-http.test.ts:351`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e2/extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile-http.test.ts#L351), [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e2))
+- **Gateway auth coverage includes the Nostr profile path:** Gateway auth tests verify `/api/channels/nostr/default/profile` returns unauthorized without bearer auth and only passes through when authenticated. ([`src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts:230`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e2/src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts#L230), [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e2))

+## Summary
+
+The reported hardcoded Google OAuth secret issue is no longer present on current `main`, and the latest shipped tag already uses an externalized Google OAuth flow instead of embedding Google OAuth client secrets in OpenClaw source. The specific bundled plugins named in the report are also no longer shipped.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Reported plugin IDs are retired:** Current config treats `google-antigravity-auth` and `google-gemini-cli-auth` as legacy removed plugin IDs, which matches the report's cited components no longer being shipped.
+  - file: [src/config/validation.ts:43](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/config/validation.ts#L43)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/config/validation.ts | sed -n '35,55p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Current Google OAuth flow does not embed a client secret:** `resolveOAuthClientConfig()` first reads `OPENCLAW_GEMINI_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID` / `_SECRET` (or `GEMINI_CLI_*`) from env, then falls back to extracting the locally installed Gemini CLI credentials; there is no hardcoded Google OAuth client secret literal in OpenClaw production code here.
+  - file: [extensions/google/oauth.credentials.ts:258](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/extensions/google/oauth.credentials.ts#L258)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/google/oauth.credentials.ts | sed -n '1,320p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Docs describe external credential sources:** User-facing docs for the shipped Google OAuth flow require a local `gemini` install and document env vars for the OAuth client id/secret rather than any bundled OpenClaw secret.
+  - file: [docs/providers/google.md:65](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/docs/providers/google.md#L65)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/providers/google.md | sed -n '50,110p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **No non-test hardcoded Google OAuth literals in current Google plugin:** A production-code search under `extensions/google` found no `GOCSPX-...` or `.apps.googleusercontent.com` literals outside tests; the only remaining matches are test fixtures and regex parsing helpers.
+  - command: `rg -n "GOCSPX-[A-Za-z0-9_-]+|apps\.googleusercontent\.com" extensions/google -g '!**/*.test.ts'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Latest shipped release already contains the non-hardcoded implementation:** Release tag `v2026.4.22` (`00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`) contains the same `extensions/google/oauth.credentials.ts` implementation that sources credentials from env or the installed Gemini CLI rather than embedding them in OpenClaw source.
+  - file: [extensions/google/oauth.credentials.ts:258](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/extensions/google/oauth.credentials.ts#L258)
+  - command: `git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:extensions/google/oauth.credentials.ts | nl -ba | sed -n '46,272p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The current `google-gemini-cli` integration still reuses Gemini CLI OAuth credentials by reading the locally installed Gemini CLI package. If maintainers want to revisit that integration design, it should be tracked separately from this specific hardcoded-secret report.
+

+# [#12545: [Security] Nostr Channel: Private Key Stored in Plaintext](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12545)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12545](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12545)
+
+Author: fr33d3m0n
+

+
+Created at: Feb 9, 2026, 10:01 UTC
+
+Updated at: Feb 20, 2026, 02:53 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)

+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))

+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main no longer stores any Nostr private key material in `extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts`; that file now persists only non-secret bus/profile metadata, so the reported plaintext-key-in-state-file condition is obsolete.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Current state-store schema contains no private key field:** `NostrBusState` and `NostrProfileState` only define timestamps, recent event IDs, last published event ID, and relay publish results. No secret/private-key field is part of either persisted shape.
+  - file: [extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts:20](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts#L20)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts | sed -n '20,66p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Current write path persists only non-secret metadata:** `writeNostrBusState` serializes `{ version, lastProcessedAt, gatewayStartedAt, recentEventIds }` and `writeNostrProfileState` serializes `{ version, lastPublishedAt, lastPublishedEventId, lastPublishResults }`, then chmods the temp file to `0600`. No private key is written here.
+  - file: [extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts:128](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts#L128)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts | sed -n '128,223p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Regression tests assert the persisted JSON shape without any key material:** The state-store tests read back only the non-secret fields above for both bus state and profile state.
+  - file: [extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.test.ts:45](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.test.ts#L45)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.test.ts | sed -n '44,189p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Released tag also has the non-secret state store:** The same non-secret `nostr-state-store.ts` contents are present in the latest release tag provided in the task context, so this is not just an unreleased main-branch change.
+  - file: [extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts:20](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts#L20)
+  - command: `git show v2026.4.22:extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts | sed -n '1,260p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+- **Docs steer operators away from config-file storage:** Nostr docs explicitly recommend `--use-env` / `${NOSTR_PRIVATE_KEY}` so the key stays in environment-backed config rather than another persisted state file.

+- Nostr private keys can still be supplied directly in config if an operator chooses `--private-key` instead of `--use-env`; that is a separate at-rest config choice, not the reported `nostr-state-store.ts` plaintext-state-file bug.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
+
+Current main no longer stores any Nostr private key material in `extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts`; that file now persists only non-secret bus/profile metadata, so the reported plaintext-key-in-state-file condition is obsolete.
+
+What I checked:
+
+- **Current state-store schema contains no private key field:** `NostrBusState` and `NostrProfileState` only define timestamps, recent event IDs, last published event ID, and relay publish results. No secret/private-key field is part of either persisted shape. ([`extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts:20`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e/extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts#L20), [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e))
+- **Current write path persists only non-secret metadata:** `writeNostrBusState` serializes `{ version, lastProcessedAt, gatewayStartedAt, recentEventIds }` and `writeNostrProfileState` serializes `{ version, lastPublishedAt, lastPublishedEventId, lastPublishResults }`, then chmods the temp file to `0600`. No private key is written here. ([`extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts:128`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e/extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts#L128), [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e))
+- **Regression tests assert the persisted JSON shape without any key material:** The state-store tests read back only the non-secret fields above for both bus state and profile state. ([`extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.test.ts:45`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e/extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.test.ts#L45), [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e))
+- **Released tag also has the non-secret state store:** The same non-secret `nostr-state-store.ts` contents are present in the latest release tag provided in the task context, so this is not just an unreleased main-branch change. ([`extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts:20`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376/extensions/nostr/src/nostr-state-store.ts#L20), [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376))
+- **Docs steer operators away from config-file storage:** Nostr docs explicitly recommend `--use-env` / `${NOSTR_PRIVATE_KEY}` so the key stays in environment-backed config rather than another persisted state file. Public docs: [`docs/channels/nostr.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/nostr). ([`docs/channels/nostr.md:39`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/nostr), [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e))
+- **Checkout remained clean:** Read-only inspection only; no repo mutations were made. ([68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e))
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.

+- **Gateway protocol exposes subscribed tool/message event streams:** The protocol docs describe `session.message` and `session.tool` as session transcript/event-stream updates for subscribed sessions.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/protocol.md:391](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/docs/gateway/protocol.md#L391)
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Gateway-backed session transcript is the documented source of truth:** Docs state the gateway-backed session transcript is the source of truth for conversations, and transcript append code persists mirrored assistant deliveries through `SessionManager.appendMessage(...)`.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/messages.md:75](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/docs/concepts/messages.md#L75)

+- **Outbound sends already fire auditing/inspection hooks:** Outbound delivery builds canonical sent-message context with channel, target, success/error, and message id for `message_sent`, and runs `message_sending` before delivery for inspection/cancellation.
+  - file: [src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts:518](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts#L518)
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Plugin hook surface explicitly supports outbound and tool interception:** Hook runner supports `message_sending`, `message_sent`, `before_tool_call`, `after_tool_call`, and synchronous `tool_result_persist` on the session-transcript hot path.
+  - file: [src/plugins/hooks.ts:733](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/plugins/hooks.ts#L733)

The issue’s core claim of 'zero controls' / unrestricted tool execution does not match the current code or shipped docs.

+  - file: [docs/gateway/sandbox-vs-tool-policy-vs-elevated.md:10](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/docs/gateway/sandbox-vs-tool-policy-vs-elevated.md#L10)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/gateway/sandbox-vs-tool-policy-vs-elevated.md | sed -n '9,70p'`

+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)

+- **Runtime filters tool list before model exposure:** The embedded runner computes `filteredBundledTools`, then builds `effectiveTools = [...tools, ...filteredBundledTools]` before allowed-tool-name collection and prompt assembly. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:731`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e2/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts#L731), [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e2))
+- **Policy uses trusted session metadata:** `applyFinalEffectiveToolPolicy` rejects untrusted group context widening and applies the full profile/global/agent/provider/group/sandbox/subagent policy pipeline to bundled tools. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/effective-tool-policy.ts:25`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e2/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/effective-tool-policy.ts#L25), [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e2))
+- **Tool calls can be blocked or approval-gated:** `runBeforeToolCallHook` can block tool execution, require an approval flow, or adjust params before `tool.execute` runs. ([`src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.ts:202`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e2/src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.ts#L202), [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e2))
+- **Hook failures are fail-closed:** The global hook runner is configured with `before_tool_call: "fail-closed"`, so hook errors block rather than silently allowing execution. ([`src/plugins/hook-runner-global.ts:32`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e2/src/plugins/hook-runner-global.ts#L32), [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e2))
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
+
+Review notes: reviewed against [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45); fix evidence: commit [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45).

+## Evidence
+
+- **Plugin hook message context still lacks session attribution fields:** `PluginHookMessageContext` on current `main` only exposes `channelId`, `accountId`, and `conversationId`; there is no `sessionKey` or `sessionId` in the plugin hook context type.
+  - file: [src/plugins/hook-message.types.ts:1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/plugins/hook-message.types.ts#L1)

+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Outbound hooks are invoked without sessionKey/sessionId in plugin context:** `applyMessageSendingHook()` passes only `{ channelId, accountId, conversationId }` to `runMessageSending`, and `createMessageSentEmitter()` builds the plugin hook context from the same reduced canonical fields. The session key is only used for internal `message:sent` hooks, not plugin hooks.
+  - file: [src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts:522](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts#L522)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts | sed -n '507,930p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Current outbound delivery still applies message_sending before chunking and emits one message_sent after chunked delivery:** `applyMessageSendingHook()` runs once per normalized payload before `sendTextChunks()`. After chunked delivery, `emitMessageSent()` is called once with the payload text, not once per actual chunk/send attempt.
+  - file: [src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts:779](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts#L779)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts | sed -n '779,919p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Tests on main assert single-call hook behavior for chunked delivery:** The chunked-delivery tests expect one internal `message:sent` event for a chunked payload, and the hook test explicitly expects `runMessageSending` to be called exactly once.
+  - file: [src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts:810](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts#L810)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts | sed -n '810,832p;1096,1135p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Gateway-mode sendMessage still drops session attribution unless mirror is present:** In `sendMessage()`, the direct path uses `requesterSessionKey ?? mirror.sessionKey`, but the gateway RPC path still sends `sessionKey: params.mirror?.sessionKey`, so non-mirrored gateway sends remain unattributed.

+# [#12660: Feature Request: Context Provider Plugin Slot (plugins.slots.context)](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12660)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12660](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12660)
+
+Author: rezabaram
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 9, 2026, 13:07 UTC

+

+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))

+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already ships this feature as the context-engine plugin slot: plugins register `api.registerContextEngine(...)`, users select it with `plugins.slots.contextEngine`, the runner replaces the model message array from `assemble(...)`, and post-turn capture runs through `afterTurn`/`ingestBatch`. The changelog says this landed in release 2026.3.7 via #22201.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **User-facing docs describe the shipped slot and purpose:** `docs/concepts/context-engine.md` says a context engine controls which messages are included for each run, can be installed as a plugin, and is selected through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`.
+  - file: [docs/concepts/context-engine.md:10](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/docs/concepts/context-engine.md#L10)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/concepts/context-engine.md | sed -n '1,320p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Manifest/config docs wire the exclusive slot:** `docs/plugins/manifest.md` documents `kind: "context-engine"` being selected through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`, and `src/config/types.plugins.ts` defines that slot in config.
+  - file: [docs/plugins/manifest.md:676](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/docs/plugins/manifest.md#L676)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/plugins/manifest.md | sed -n '650,710p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Public plugin API exposes context-engine registration:** `src/plugins/types.ts` includes `registerContextEngine(id, factory)` as an exclusive slot registration API for plugins.
+  - file: [src/plugins/types.ts:2005](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/src/plugins/types.ts#L2005)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/types.ts | sed -n '1990,2035p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Runtime actually replaces prompt history from plugin output:** `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts` calls `assemble(...)` with the full message list and overwrites `activeSession.agent.state.messages` with the engine-returned messages before model execution.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:1636](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts#L1636)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts | sed -n '1636,1675p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Post-turn capture lifecycle is implemented:** `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.context-engine-helpers.ts` runs `afterTurn(...)` when available, otherwise falls back to `ingestBatch(...)` or per-message `ingest(...)`, matching the requested capture hook behavior.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.context-engine-helpers.ts:238](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.context-engine-helpers.ts#L238)

+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Changelog records the feature landing:** `CHANGELOG.md` entry for release `2026.3.7` says OpenClaw added the `ContextEngine` plugin slot with full lifecycle hooks and config-driven resolution, explicitly citing PR `#22201`.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:2878](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/CHANGELOG.md#L2878)
+  - command: `nl -ba CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '2874,2879p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)

+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The shipped config key is `plugins.slots.contextEngine`, not the issue's proposed `plugins.slots.context`.
+- The shipped method names are `assemble` and `afterTurn`/`ingestBatch`, not the exact sketch names `buildContext` and `captureExchange`, but the behavior is present.

+Current `main` already ships this feature as the context-engine plugin slot: plugins register `api.registerContextEngine(...)`, users select it with `plugins.slots.contextEngine`, the runner replaces the model message array from `assemble(...)`, and post-turn capture runs through `afterTurn`/`ingestBatch`. The changelog says this landed in release 2026.3.7 via #22201.

+What I checked:

+- **User-facing docs describe the shipped slot and purpose:** [`docs/concepts/context-engine.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/context-engine) says a context engine controls which messages are included for each run, can be installed as a plugin, and is selected through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`. ([`docs/concepts/context-engine.md:10`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/context-engine), [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e))
+- **Manifest/config docs wire the exclusive slot:** [`docs/plugins/manifest.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/manifest) documents `kind: "context-engine"` being selected through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`, and [`src/config/types.plugins.ts`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e/src/config/types.plugins.ts) defines that slot in config. ([`docs/plugins/manifest.md:676`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/manifest), [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e))
+- **Public plugin API exposes context-engine registration:** [`src/plugins/types.ts`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e/src/plugins/types.ts) includes `registerContextEngine(id, factory)` as an exclusive slot registration API for plugins. ([`src/plugins/types.ts:2005`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e/src/plugins/types.ts#L2005), [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e))
+- **Runtime actually replaces prompt history from plugin output:** [`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts) calls `assemble(...)` with the full message list and overwrites `activeSession.agent.state.messages` with the engine-returned messages before model execution. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:1636`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts#L1636), [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e))
+- **Post-turn capture lifecycle is implemented:** [`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.context-engine-helpers.ts`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.context-engine-helpers.ts) runs `afterTurn(...)` when available, otherwise falls back to `ingestBatch(...)` or per-message `ingest(...)`, matching the requested capture hook behavior. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.context-engine-helpers.ts:238`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.context-engine-helpers.ts#L238), [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e))
+- **Changelog records the feature landing:** [`CHANGELOG.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e/CHANGELOG.md) entry for release `2026.3.7` says OpenClaw added the `ContextEngine` plugin slot with full lifecycle hooks and config-driven resolution, explicitly citing PR `#22201`. ([`CHANGELOG.md:2878`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e/CHANGELOG.md#L2878), [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e))
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.

+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12739](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12739)
+

+

+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already makes `/status` derive its denominator from the active provider/model context window rather than only the global session/default `contextTokens`, with regression coverage for model-switch and fallback cases.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Status recomputes displayed context from selected/active model:** `buildStatusMessage` resolves both selected and active model context windows, then chooses the active/provider-specific limit for the rendered `Context:` line instead of blindly echoing the stored/global token cap.
+  - file: [src/status/status-message.ts:594](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/f04a3dced01bba230390f570f2369e3f2b63b3e7/src/status/status-message.ts#L594)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/status/status-message.ts | sed -n '590,716p'`
+  - sha: [f04a3dced01b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/f04a3dced01bba230390f570f2369e3f2b63b3e7)
+- **Provider-qualified model lookup prefers per-model config:** `resolveContextTokensForModel` directly scans `models.providers[provider].models[].contextWindow/contextTokens` and prefers provider-qualified cache keys so `/status` gets the correct per-model window.
+  - file: [src/agents/context.ts:435](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/f04a3dced01bba230390f570f2369e3f2b63b3e7/src/agents/context.ts#L435)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/context.ts | sed -n '435,507p'`
+  - sha: [f04a3dced01b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/f04a3dced01bba230390f570f2369e3f2b63b3e7)
+- **Regression test covers switching away from a smaller model override:** Test starts with a session persisted at `contextTokens: 4096`, switches the session model to `large-model`, and asserts status now shows `Context: 1.0k/66k`, matching the active model instead of the stale smaller value.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/status.test.ts:588](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/f04a3dced01bba230390f570f2369e3f2b63b3e7/src/auto-reply/status.test.ts#L588)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/status.test.ts | sed -n '588,619p'`
+  - sha: [f04a3dced01b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/f04a3dced01bba230390f570f2369e3f2b63b3e7)
+- **Regression test covers stale larger window after fallback/model change:** Test seeds a stale `contextTokens: 1_048_576` value, marks the active runtime model as `minimax-portal/MiniMax-M2.7` with `contextWindow: 200_000`, and asserts status shows `Context: 49k/200k` and not `49k/1.0m`.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/status.test.ts:621](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/f04a3dced01bba230390f570f2369e3f2b63b3e7/src/auto-reply/status.test.ts#L621)

+  - sha: [f04a3dced01b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/f04a3dced01bba230390f570f2369e3f2b63b3e7)
+

+- none

([`src/agents/context.ts:435`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/f04a3dced01b/src/agents/context.ts#L435), [f04a3dced01b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/f04a3dced01b))

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already coalesces forwarded Telegram text+media bursts into one inbound turn, with shipped changelog coverage and a regression test for the exact reported flow.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Forward-burst debounce lane on current main:** `extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts:213` creates the inbound debouncer; `:215-216` gives forwarded messages a dedicated debounce window; `:225-229` explicitly debounces media-only forwarded entries; `:254-282` merges text and media from multiple forwarded entries into one synthetic message before `processMessage`.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts:213](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts#L213)

+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Regression test matches the report:** `extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.downloads-media-file-path-no-file-download.e2e.test.ts:421-467` asserts that a forwarded text message followed by a forwarded photo is processed as exactly one turn, and verifies both text and media are present in the single payload.
+  - file: [extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.downloads-media-file-path-no-file-download.e2e.test.ts:421](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.downloads-media-file-path-no-file-download.e2e.test.ts#L421)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.downloads-media-file-path-no-file-download.e2e.test.ts | sed -n '413,470p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Shipped release note:** The `v2026.4.22` release commit includes `CHANGELOG.md:4178`: "Telegram/Forward bursts: coalesce forwarded text+media updates through a dedicated forward lane debounce window... (#19476)".

+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions

+## Decision

+Close: already implemented on main

+Confidence: high

+Action taken: proposed_close

+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already forwards `accountId` into plugin command execution and exposes it on `PluginCommandContext`, and the latest tagged release `v2026.4.22` contains the same implementation.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Plugin command handler forwards AccountId:** `handlePluginCommand` passes `params.ctx.AccountId` into `executePluginCommand`.
+  - file: [src/auto-reply/reply/commands-plugin.ts:50](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/src/auto-reply/reply/commands-plugin.ts#L50)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/auto-reply/reply/commands-plugin.ts | sed -n '1,120p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Plugin command executor accepts and forwards accountId:** `executePluginCommand` accepts `accountId` in params and assigns `ctx.accountId` from the effective resolved account.
+  - file: [src/plugins/commands.ts:186](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/src/plugins/commands.ts#L186)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/commands.ts | sed -n '150,280p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Public command context includes accountId:** `PluginCommandContext` explicitly defines `accountId?: string` for plugin handlers.
+  - file: [src/plugins/types.ts:1714](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/src/plugins/types.ts#L1714)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/types.ts | sed -n '1678,1738p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Regression coverage for account propagation exists:** Test asserts plugin command handlers receive the effective default account id when no explicit accountId is passed.
+  - file: [src/plugins/commands.test.ts:618](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/src/plugins/commands.test.ts#L618)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/plugins/commands.test.ts | sed -n '540,730p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Latest release already contains the implementation:** The tagged release commit `v2026.4.22` shows the same `accountId` param and `ctx.accountId` assignment in `src/plugins/commands.ts`, so this was shipped in that release.
+  - file: [src/plugins/commands.ts:186](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/plugins/commands.ts#L186)
+  - command: `git show 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5:src/plugins/commands.ts | nl -ba | sed -n '171,235p'`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)

+

+- I did not run runtime reproduction or tests because this review is read-only, so the conclusion is based on source, tagged-release inspection, and existing test coverage.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because this is already implemented on current `main`, and the same implementation is present in the shipped release `v2026.4.22`. `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-plugin.ts:50` forwards `params.ctx.AccountId` into `executePluginCommand`, `src/plugins/commands.ts:186` accepts `accountId` and `src/plugins/commands.ts:229` places it onto `ctx.accountId`, and `src/plugins/types.ts:1714` includes `accountId` on `PluginCommandContext`. There is also regression coverage in `src/plugins/commands.test.ts:618`. If you still see `ctx.accountId` as `undefined` on `v2026.4.22` or newer, please open a new issue with exact reproduction steps, channel/plugin details, and the version you tested.
