+# [#12838: Feature Request: Auto-resize large images before LLM submission](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12838)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12838](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/12838)
+
+Author: Knytro23
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 9, 2026, 19:51 UTC
+
+Updated at: Feb 10, 2026, 06:57 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: commit [f04a3dced01b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/f04a3dced01bba230390f570f2369e3f2b63b3e7)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already auto-sanitizes replayed image payloads before provider submission, including downscaling/recompressing oversized images to fit byte limits, dropping images that still cannot fit, and converting residual provider image-size failures into a friendly user-facing message instead of dumping the raw upstream error.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Session history is sanitized before replay:** `sanitizeSessionMessagesImages(...)` is applied to normalized replay history before provider submission, so historical user/assistant/tool images are rewritten on the send path.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts:465](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts#L465)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts | sed -n '460,478p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Default image limit matches the reported Anthropic ceiling:** Image sanitization defaults to `5 * 1024 * 1024` bytes, aligning with the issue's cited 5 MB per-image limit.
+  - file: [src/agents/image-sanitization.ts:9](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/agents/image-sanitization.ts#L9)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/image-sanitization.ts | sed -n '1,20p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Oversized images are auto-resized and recompressed:** `resizeImageBase64IfNeeded(...)` converts oversized image blocks to JPEG and iterates size/quality combinations until the payload fits `maxBytes`.
+  - file: [src/agents/tool-images.ts:148](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/agents/tool-images.ts#L148)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/tool-images.ts | sed -n '148,240p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Failed resizes fall back to omission text instead of raw image payloads:** If an image still cannot be reduced below the limit, sanitization catches the error and replaces the block with an omission note.
+  - file: [src/agents/tool-images.ts:326](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/agents/tool-images.ts#L326)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/tool-images.ts | sed -n '244,334p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **User-facing error text is now sanitized:** Residual provider image-size errors are intercepted and turned into `Image too large for the model ... Please compress or resize the image and try again.` rather than echoing the raw upstream rejection into chat.
+  - file: [src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts:1297](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts#L1297)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts | sed -n '1294,1315p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Regression test covers byte-limit shrinking:** The test suite asserts that oversized PNG image blocks are shrunk below the configured byte limit and rewritten as JPEG.
+  - file: [src/agents/tool-images.test.ts:27](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/agents/tool-images.test.ts#L27)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/agents/tool-images.test.ts | sed -n '27,66p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Implementation landed on main after the latest release:** Commit `f04a3dced01bba230390f570f2369e3f2b63b3e7` introduced the image sanitization path and changelog entry, and it is an ancestor of `HEAD` but not contained in tag `v2026.4.22`.
+  - file: [CHANGELOG.md:6013](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/f04a3dced01bba230390f570f2369e3f2b63b3e7/CHANGELOG.md#L6013)
+  - command: `git merge-base --is-ancestor f04a3dced01bba230390f570f2369e3f2b63b3e7 HEAD; git merge-base --is-ancestor f04a3dced01bba230390f570f2369e3f2b63b3e7 00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5; git blame -L 6013,6013 -- CHANGELOG.md`
+  - sha: [f04a3dced01b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/f04a3dced01bba230390f570f2369e3f2b63b3e7)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The fix appears to be on `main` but not in the latest tagged release `v2026.4.22`, so users on released builds may still encounter the old behavior until a newer release ships.
+- This was a code/history review only; no live Discord reproduction was run under the read-only constraints.
+
+## Close Comment

+# [#13059: [Feature] Publish Browser Relay extension to Chrome Web Store](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/13059)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/13059](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/13059)
+
+Author: Koul91
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 10, 2026, 02:48 UTC
+
+Updated at: Feb 10, 2026, 06:54 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: not determined
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: cannot reproduce on current main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Issue #13059 is obsolete on current `main`. OpenClaw no longer uses a Chrome extension relay for its browser feature, so there is no Browser Relay extension left to publish to the Chrome Web Store.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Current browser docs say the managed `openclaw` profile does not require an extension:** The browser docs describe the built-in browser feature as an OpenClaw-managed browser/control service and explicitly state `openclaw` is a managed isolated browser with "no extension required."
+  - file: [docs/tools/browser.md:82](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/docs/tools/browser.md#L82)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/tools/browser.md | sed -n '1,140p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Extension relay lifecycle hook is now a stub:** `ensureExtensionRelayForProfiles` is intentionally a no-op because "the Chrome extension relay path has been removed."
+  - file: [extensions/browser/src/browser/server-lifecycle.ts:13](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/extensions/browser/src/browser/server-lifecycle.ts#L13)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/browser/src/browser/server-lifecycle.ts | sed -n '1,120p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Browser config no longer creates a `chrome-relay` profile:** Current browser config tests assert that `chrome-relay` is no longer auto-created on default config and derived-port variants.
+  - file: [extensions/browser/src/browser/config.test.ts:57](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/extensions/browser/src/browser/config.test.ts#L57)
+  - command: `nl -ba extensions/browser/src/browser/config.test.ts | sed -n '1,110p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Doctor guidance treats extension-based browser setup as legacy and migrates away from it:** The gateway doctor docs say `browser.profiles.*.driver: "extension"` is normalized to `"existing-session"` and `browser.relayBindHost` is removed because the Chrome extension path is removed.
+  - file: [docs/gateway/doctor.md:207](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/docs/gateway/doctor.md#L207)
+  - command: `nl -ba docs/gateway/doctor.md | sed -n '180,220p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Legacy-config normalizer removes extension relay settings in code:** `normalizeLegacyBrowserConfig` deletes `browser.relayBindHost` and rewrites `driver: "extension"` to `driver: "existing-session"`, with change messages calling the old Chrome extension relay setting legacy.
+  - file: [src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-core-normalizers.ts:27](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-core-normalizers.ts#L27)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-core-normalizers.ts | sed -n '1,80p'`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **No current docs or commands reference `openclaw browser extension install` or `/tools/chrome-extension`:** A repo-wide search on `docs`, `src`, and `extensions` finds no current Browser Relay install command or Chrome-extension doc surface, matching the removal of that flow.
+  - command: `rg -n "browser extension install|tools/chrome-extension|chrome-extension" docs src extensions README.md`
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- This conclusion is based on current `main` only; I could not inspect GitHub cross-links because `gh` is unauthenticated in this environment (`GH_TOKEN` missing).
+- There may still be a separate desire for a Chrome Web Store companion in the future, but that would be a new product request, not the legacy Browser Relay problem described here.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing as a Codex review because the issue no longer matches current OpenClaw behavior. On current `main`, the browser feature no longer depends on a Chrome extension relay: `docs/tools/browser.md:82` says the managed `openclaw` profile needs no extension, `extensions/browser/src/browser/server-lifecycle.ts:13` says the Chrome extension relay path has been removed, and current config/tests assert that `chrome-relay` is no longer created (`extensions/browser/src/browser/config.test.ts:57`). The doctor/migration path also rewrites legacy `driver: "extension"` configs to `existing-session` and removes `browser.relayBindHost` (`docs/gateway/doctor.md:207`, `src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-core-normalizers.ts:27`). Since there is no current Browser Relay extension to publish, this specific request is obsolete. If there is still a current browser distribution/onboarding problem with the managed browser plugin, please open a new issue with the exact current flow, expected behavior, and docs/CLI surface involved.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb). Specific fixed release/commit was not determined by this review.

+## Evidence

+# [#13133: Enhancement: Short-circuit --version in bootstrap to avoid full app initialization](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/13133)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/13133](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/13133)
+
+Author: rocketman-code
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 10, 2026, 04:45 UTC
+
+Updated at: Feb 10, 2026, 06:53 UTC
+
+Reviewed against: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already short-circuits root `--version` before full CLI initialization, skips the respawn path for help/version invocations, and the same implementation is present in release `v2026.4.22`.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Entry now fast-paths root version before heavy startup:** `src/entry.ts:128` calls `tryHandleRootVersionFastPath(process.argv)` and only falls through to `runMainOrRootHelp(process.argv)` when that returns false.
+  - file: [src/entry.ts:128](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/entry.ts#L128)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/entry.ts | sed -n '100,130p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Dedicated root version handler prints and exits:** `src/entry.version-fast-path.ts:18-23` recognizes root `--version`, then `src/entry.version-fast-path.ts:45-50` resolves the version/commit and exits with code 0 without loading the main Commander program.
+  - file: [src/entry.version-fast-path.ts:18](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/entry.version-fast-path.ts#L18)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/entry.version-fast-path.ts | sed -n '18,50p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Respawn is skipped for help/version invocations:** `src/entry.respawn.ts:38-39` returns `null` when `shouldSkipRespawnForArgv(argv)` is true, and `src/cli/respawn-policy.ts:3-4` defines that skip in terms of `resolveCliArgvInvocation(argv).hasHelpOrVersion`.
+  - file: [src/entry.respawn.ts:38](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/entry.respawn.ts#L38)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/entry.respawn.ts | sed -n '32,40p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Regression tests cover the fast path and respawn skip:** `src/entry.version-fast-path.test.ts:34-43` asserts version output + exit, and `src/cli/cli-utils.test.ts:20-29` asserts help/version argv skip respawn while normal commands do not.
+  - file: [src/entry.version-fast-path.test.ts:34](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45/src/entry.version-fast-path.test.ts#L34)
+  - command: `nl -ba src/entry.version-fast-path.test.ts | sed -n '34,43p'; nl -ba src/cli/cli-utils.test.ts | sed -n '20,29p'`
+  - sha: [3361593442e2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45)
+- **Implementation is already in the latest shipped release:** `git show v2026.4.22:src/entry.ts`, `git show v2026.4.22:src/entry.version-fast-path.ts`, and `git diff --name-only v2026.4.22 -- src/entry.ts src/entry.version-fast-path.ts src/entry.respawn.ts src/cli/respawn-policy.ts src/entry.version-fast-path.test.ts src/cli/cli-utils.test.ts` show the same fast-path implementation in release `v2026.4.22`.
+  - command: `git diff --name-only v2026.4.22 -- src/entry.ts src/entry.version-fast-path.ts src/entry.respawn.ts src/cli/respawn-policy.ts src/entry.version-fast-path.test.ts src/cli/cli-utils.test.ts`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- I did not execute `openclaw --version` locally because this review is read-only and the environment is missing normal repo tooling (`pnpm` was unavailable), so this conclusion is based on code and shipped-tag inspection rather than runtime timing.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing as implemented on current `main`. Codex review verified that `src/entry.ts:128` now short-circuits root `--version` through `tryHandleRootVersionFastPath()`, `src/entry.version-fast-path.ts:18-50` prints the version and exits without loading the full CLI, and `src/entry.respawn.ts:38-39` plus `src/cli/respawn-policy.ts:3-4` skip the respawn path for help/version invocations. The same implementation is present in shipped release `v2026.4.22` (`00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`), so this enhancement has already landed.

+
+Closing as implemented on current `main` after Codex review. OpenClaw already supports per-agent identity through `agents.list[].identity` (including `name` and `avatar`) in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md:1763`, and the CLI explicitly writes those fields via `openclaw agents set-identity` in `docs/cli/agents.md:163`. The Control UI resolves the displayed assistant identity from the selected agent in `src/gateway/assistant-identity.ts:81`, and gateway/session payloads expose that identity data in `src/gateway/session-utils.ts:688`. Current `main` at `3361593442e295e9bb1f55cbfb38861ea9413e45` therefore already covers the requested per-agent name/avatar behavior, even though the shipped schema is `agents.list[].identity` rather than the proposed `ui.agents` shape.

+# [#13238: Proposal: Define a release quality gate and canary policy for stable tags](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/13238)
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: [https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/13238](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/13238)
+
+Author: 0xRaini
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Created at: Feb 10, 2026, 07:15 UTC
+
+Updated at: Feb 27, 2026, 14:05 UTC
+

+
+Latest release at review time: [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22) ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+
+Fixed in: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Decision
+
+Close: already implemented on main
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: proposed_close
+
+## Summary
+
+Current `main` already has a documented beta-first stable release policy plus enforced release preflight and release-check workflows, which covers the requested stable quality gate/canary process well enough.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Public release policy documents beta-first stable promotion and release checklist:** `docs/reference/RELEASING.md` defines beta-first stable releases, says stable follows only after the latest beta is validated, and lists the required release preflight/check steps and promotion flow from `beta` to `latest`.
+  - file: [docs/reference/RELEASING.md:33](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/docs/reference/RELEASING.md#L33)
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Stable publish path is gated by preflight:** `.github/workflows/openclaw-npm-release.yml` requires validation-only preflight, runs `pnpm check`, `pnpm check:test-types`, `pnpm check:architecture`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm ui:build`, `pnpm release:check`, and requires `preflight_run_id` for the real publish path.
+  - file: [.github/workflows/openclaw-npm-release.yml:107](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/.github/workflows/openclaw-npm-release.yml#L107)
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Separate release-check workflow covers cross-OS and live QA lanes:** `.github/workflows/openclaw-release-checks.yml` validates a release ref and runs install smoke, cross-OS release checks, live/e2e suites, QA parity, and live Matrix/Telegram release lanes before approval.
+  - file: [.github/workflows/openclaw-release-checks.yml:129](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/.github/workflows/openclaw-release-checks.yml#L129)
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Cross-OS fresh/upgrade validation exists for release candidates:** `.github/workflows/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks-reusable.yml` exposes `fresh|upgrade|both` modes and baseline-version inputs for installer/dev-update and packaged upgrade validation.
+  - file: [.github/workflows/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks-reusable.yml:25](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb/.github/workflows/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks-reusable.yml#L25)
+  - sha: [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb)
+- **Shipped release already contains the policy and workflows:** The latest tagged release `v2026.4.22` contains the same release-policy doc and release workflows, so this is not just unshipped work on `main`.
+  - command: `git show v2026.4.22:docs/reference/RELEASING.md ; git show v2026.4.22:.github/workflows/openclaw-release-checks.yml ; git show v2026.4.22:.github/workflows/openclaw-npm-release.yml`
+  - sha: [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5)
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The implementation is workflow- and docs-driven rather than a dedicated release PR template, so the proposal is satisfied by process/pipeline changes rather than the exact mechanism suggested in the issue.
+- Public docs describe beta-first promotion and release gates, but any finer rollback/go-no-go criteria may live in maintainer-only release docs.
+
+## Close Comment
+
+Closing from a Codex review because this is already implemented on current `main`, and the public release tag `v2026.4.22` already contains it. `docs/reference/RELEASING.md` now documents a beta-first stable policy, required release preflight steps, and promotion from `beta` to `latest`; `.github/workflows/openclaw-npm-release.yml` enforces the gated preflight/publish flow with `preflight_run_id`; and `.github/workflows/openclaw-release-checks.yml` runs install smoke, cross-OS validation, QA parity, and live Matrix/Telegram release lanes before approval. The checklist/policy ended up landing in release docs and workflows rather than a dedicated release PR template, but the requested stable quality gate and canary process are in place well enough now.
+Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [68e2d6f0889e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/68e2d6f0889e5365ecdc8f01ab5e3bf6065cbeeb). Fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).
